revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 八坂神社 |画像 = [[画像:IMG_tomosumi.JPG|250px]] |所在地 = 長崎県南松浦郡新上五島町友住郷字立木353 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|32|59|54.7|N|129|10|27.2|E|scale:25000}} |祭神 = 素戔男尊 外4柱 |社格 = 旧無格社 |創建 = 不詳 |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 =11月3日 |神事 = 有川神楽（例大祭） }}
'''八坂神社'''（やさかじんじゃ）は、[[長崎県]][[新上五島町]]友住郷に鎮座する[[神社]]である。同郷の小高い山の頂上付近に鎮座し、麓の住宅地には[[下社]]がある。
== 祭神 == [[スサノオ|素戔男尊]]を主祭神に、[[大物主神]]、[[金山彦神]]、[[事代主神]]、 [[大山祇神]]の4柱を[[配祀]]する。
配祀神中の大物主神・金山彦神は金刀比羅神社の、事代主神は白峯神社の、大山祇神は大山神社の祭神である。 [[ファイル:IMG_tomosumi1.JPG|240px|thumb|社殿]]
== 歴史 == 創建年代、及び由緒等は不詳。
明治7年（[[1874年]]）5月5日、[[無格社]]に列せられる。同41年(((1908年]]）12月4日、同郷鎮座の[[金刀比羅神社]]を[[合祀]]。また、年代は不祥だが同郷の白峯神社・大山神社も合祀された。 [[ファイル:IMG_tomosumi2.JPG|240px|thumb|金刀比羅神社]]
== 祭祀 == 11月2、3日の例祭には[[有川神楽]]が奉納される。有川神楽は国の[[選択無形民俗文化財]]にも指定された[[五島神楽]]に含まれる。
;主な祭礼・神事 *[[祈年祭]]（2月18日） *[[例大祭]]（11月2、3日） *[[新嘗祭]]（11月29日）
== 境内社 == [[ファイル:IMG_tomosumi3.JPG|240px|thumb|境内社・白峯神社]] *[[白峯神社]]
== その他の神社 == その他の友住郷の神社に'''恵比須神社'''、'''行者山社'''がある。
また、隣接する赤尾郷に'''[[孕神社]]'''が、江ノ浜郷に'''[[江ノ濱神社]]'''がある。 <gallery> 画像:IMG_ tomosumi4.JPG|行者山社 画像:IMG_harami.JPG|孕神社 画像:IMG_enohama.JPG|江ノ濱神社（江ノ浜郷）</gallery>
== 参考文献 == *『[[有川町]]郷土誌 平成6年』有川町郷土史編纂委員会 *『五島神社誌 昭和18年（復刻版）』神祇会南松浦郡部著
{{DEFAULTSORT:やさかしんしや}} [[Category:長崎県の神社]] [[Category:長崎県の旧無格社]] [[Category:新上五島町の神社]] {{shinto-stub}}

{{神社 |名称 = 八坂神社 |画像 = [[画像:IMG_tomosumi.JPG|250px]] |所在地 = 長崎県南松浦郡新上五島町友住郷字立木353 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|32|59|54.7|N|129|10|27.2|E|scale:25000}} |祭神 = 素戔男尊 外4柱 |社格 = 旧無格社 |創建 = 不詳 |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 =11月3日 |神事 = 有川神楽（例大祭） }}

;主な祭礼・神事 :*[[祈年祭]]（2月18日） :*[[例大祭]]（11月2、3日） :*[[新嘗祭]]（11月29日）

== 参考文献 == *『[[有川町]]郷土誌 平成6年』有川町郷土史編纂委員会 *『五島神社誌 昭和18年（復刻版）』神祇会南松浦郡部著

revision.comment: 文化財用語用法。選択無形民俗文化財は指定ではなく選択。
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 八坂神社 |画像 = [[File:IMG_tomosumi.JPG|250px]] |所在地 = 長崎県南松浦郡新上五島町友住郷字立木353 |位置 = {{Coord|32|59|56.0|N|129|10|32.7|E|type:landmark_region:JP-42|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = 素盞男神 |社格 = 旧無格社 |創建 = 不詳 |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = 11月3日 |神事 = 有川神楽（例大祭） |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Nagasaki Prefecture|label=八坂神社|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }} '''八坂神社'''（やさかじんじゃ）は、[[長崎県]][[新上五島町]]友住郷に鎮座する[[神社]]である。同郷の小高い山の頂上付近に鎮座し、麓の住宅地には[[下社]]がある。
== 祭神 == [[スサノオ|素盞男神]]を主祭神に、[[大物主神]]、[[金山彦神]]、[[事代主神]]、 [[大山祇神]]の4柱を[[配祀]]する。
配祀神中の大物主神・金山彦神は金刀比羅神社の、事代主神は白峯神社の、大山祇神は大山神社の祭神である。
== 歴史 == [[ファイル:IMG_tomosumi1.JPG|180px|thumb|社殿]] 創建年代、及び由緒等は不詳。
[[明治]]7年（[[1874年]]）5月5日、[[無格社]]に列せられる。明治41年（[[1908年]]）12月4日、同郷鎮座の[[金刀比羅神社]]を[[合祀]]。また、年代は不詳だが同郷の白峯神社・大山神社も合祀された。
== 祭祀 == 11月2、3日の例祭には[[有川神楽]]が奉納される。有川神楽は国の[[選択無形民俗文化財]]にも選択された[[五島神楽]]に含まれる。
;主な祭礼・神事 :*[[祈年祭]]（2月18日） :*[[例大祭]]（11月2、3日） :*[[新嘗祭]]（11月29日）
{{clear}} == 境内社 == [[ファイル:IMG_tomosumi2.JPG|180px|thumb|金刀比羅神社]] [[ファイル:IMG_tomosumi3.JPG|180px|thumb|境内社・鎌倉神社]] *鎌倉神社
{{clear}}
== その他の神社 == その他の友住郷の神社に'''恵比須神社'''、'''行者山社'''がある。また、隣接する赤尾郷に'''[[孕神社]]'''が、江ノ浜郷に'''[[江ノ濱神社]]'''がある。 <gallery> File:IMG_tomosumi4.JPG|行者山社 File:IMG_harami.JPG|孕神社 File:IMG_enohama.JPG|江ノ濱神社（江ノ浜郷） </gallery>
== 参考文献 == *『[[有川町]]郷土誌 平成6年』有川町郷土史編纂委員会 *『五島神社誌 昭和18年（復刻版）』神祇会南松浦郡部著
{{shinto-stub}}
{{DEFAULTSORT:やさかしんしや}} [[Category:八坂神社]] [[Category:新上五島町の神社]] [[Category:祇園神社]] [[Category:長崎県の旧無格社]]

revision.comment: Removing from [[Category:pages needing attribution]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 八坂神社 |画像 = [[File:IMG_tomosumi.JPG|250px]] |所在地 = 長崎県南松浦郡新上五島町友住郷字立木353 |位置 = {{Coord|32|59|56.0|N|129|10|32.7|E|type:landmark_region:JP-42|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = 素盞男神 |社格 = 旧無格社 |創建 = 不詳 |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = 11月3日 |神事 = 有川神楽（例大祭） |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Nagasaki Prefecture|label=八坂神社|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }} '''八坂神社'''（やさかじんじゃ）は、[[長崎県]][[新上五島町]]友住郷に鎮座する[[神社]]である。同郷の小高い山の頂上付近に鎮座し、麓の住宅地には[[下社]]がある。
== 祭神 == [[スサノオ|素盞男神]]を主祭神に、[[大物主神]]、[[金山彦神]]、[[事代主神]]、 [[大山祇神]]の4柱を[[配祀]]する。
配祀神中の大物主神・金山彦神は金刀比羅神社の、事代主神は白峯神社の、大山祇神は大山神社の祭神である。
== 歴史 == [[ファイル:IMG_tomosumi1.JPG|180px|thumb|社殿]] 創建年代、及び由緒等は不詳。
[[明治]]7年（[[1874年]]）5月5日、[[無格社]]に列せられる。明治41年（[[1908年]]）12月4日、同郷鎮座の[[金刀比羅神社]]を[[合祀]]。また、年代は不詳だが同郷の白峯神社・大山神社も合祀された。
== 祭祀 == 11月2、3日の例祭には[[有川神楽]]が奉納される。有川神楽は国の[[選択無形民俗文化財]]にも選択された[[五島神楽]]に含まれる。
;主な祭礼・神事 :*[[祈年祭]]（2月18日） :*[[例大祭]]（11月2、3日） :*[[新嘗祭]]（11月29日）
{{clear}} == 境内社 == [[ファイル:IMG_tomosumi2.JPG|180px|thumb|金刀比羅神社]] [[ファイル:IMG_tomosumi3.JPG|180px|thumb|境内社・鎌倉神社]] *鎌倉神社
{{clear}}
== その他の神社 == その他の友住郷の神社に'''恵比須神社'''、'''行者山社'''がある。また、隣接する赤尾郷に'''[[孕神社]]'''が、江ノ浜郷に'''[[江ノ濱神社]]'''がある。 <gallery> File:IMG_tomosumi4.JPG|行者山社 File:IMG_harami.JPG|孕神社 File:IMG_enohama.JPG|江ノ濱神社（江ノ浜郷） </gallery>
== 参考文献 == *『[[有川町]]郷土誌 平成6年』有川町郷土史編纂委員会 *『五島神社誌 昭和18年（復刻版）』神祇会南松浦郡部著
{{shinto-stub}}
{{DEFAULTSORT:やさかしんしや}} [[Category:八坂神社]] [[Category:新上五島町の神社]] [[Category:祇園神社]] [[Category:長崎県の旧無格社]] [[ja:八坂神社 (新上五島町友住郷)]][[Category:New pages]]

== 祭神 == [[スサノオ|素盞男神]]を主祭神に、[[大物主神]]、[[金山彦神]]、[[事代主神]]、 [[大山祇神]]の4柱を[[配祀]]する。
配祀神中の大物主神・金山彦神は金刀比羅神社の、事代主神は白峯神社の、大山祇神は大山神社の祭神である。
== 歴史 == [[ファイル:IMG_tomosumi1.JPG|180px|thumb|社殿]] 創建年代、及び由緒等は不詳。
[[明治]]7年（[[1874年]]）5月5日、[[無格社]]に列せられる。明治41年（[[1908年]]）12月4日、同郷鎮座の[[金刀比羅神社]]を[[合祀]]。また、年代は不詳だが同郷の白峯神社・大山神社も合祀された。
== 祭祀 == 11月2、3日の例祭には[[有川神楽]]が奉納される。有川神楽は国の[[選択無形民俗文化財]]にも選択された[[五島神楽]]に含まれる。
;主な祭礼・神事 :*[[祈年祭]]（2月18日） :*[[例大祭]]（11月2、3日） :*[[新嘗祭]]（11月29日）
{{clear}} == 境内社 == [[ファイル:IMG_tomosumi2.JPG|180px|thumb|金刀比羅神社]] [[ファイル:IMG_tomosumi3.JPG|180px|thumb|境内社・鎌倉神社]] *鎌倉神社

== 参考文献 == *『[[有川町]]郷土誌 平成6年』有川町郷土史編纂委員会 *『五島神社誌 昭和18年（復刻版）』神祇会南松浦郡部著

{{神社 |名称 = 八坂神社 |画像 = [[File:IMG_tomosumi.JPG|250px]] |所在地 = 長崎県南松浦郡新上五島町友住郷字立木353 |位置 = {{Coord|32|59|56.0|N|129|10|32.7|E|type:landmark_region:JP-42|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = 素盞男神 |社格 = 旧無格社 |創建 = 不詳 |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = 11月3日 |神事 = 有川神楽（例大祭） |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Nagasaki Prefecture|label=八坂神社|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }} '''八坂神社'''（やさかじんじゃ）は、[[長崎県]][[新上五島町]]友住郷に鎮座する[[神社]]である。同郷の小高い山の頂上付近に鎮座し、麓の住宅地には[[下社]]がある。
== 祭神 == [[スサノオ|素盞男神]]を主祭神に、[[大物主神]]、[[金山彦神]]、[[事代主神]]、 [[大山祇神]]の4柱を[[配祀]]する。
配祀神中の大物主神・金山彦神は金刀比羅神社の、事代主神は白峯神社の、大山祇神は大山神社の祭神である。
== 歴史 == [[File:IMG_tomosumi1.JPG|180px|thumb|社殿]] 創建年代、及び由緒等は不詳。
[[明治]]7年（[[1874年]]）5月5日、[[無格社]]に列せられる。明治41年（[[1908年]]）12月4日、同郷鎮座の[[金刀比羅神社]]を[[合祀]]。また、年代は不詳だが同郷の白峯神社・大山神社も合祀された。
== 祭祀 == 11月2、3日の例祭には[[有川神楽]]が奉納される。有川神楽は国の[[選択無形民俗文化財]]にも選択された[[五島神楽]]に含まれる。
;主な祭礼・神事 :*[[祈年祭]]（2月18日） :*[[例大祭]]（11月2、3日） :*[[新嘗祭]]（11月29日）
{{clear}} == 境内社 == [[File:IMG_tomosumi2.JPG|180px|thumb|金刀比羅神社]] [[File:IMG_tomosumi3.JPG|180px|thumb|境内社・鎌倉神社]] *鎌倉神社
{{clear}}
== その他の神社 == その他の友住郷の神社に'''恵比須神社'''、'''行者山社'''がある。また、隣接する赤尾郷に'''[[孕神社]]'''が、江ノ浜郷に'''[[江ノ濱神社]]'''がある。 <gallery> File:IMG_tomosumi4.JPG|行者山社 File:IMG_harami.JPG|孕神社 File:IMG_enohama.JPG|江ノ濱神社（江ノ浜郷） </gallery>

{{Shinto Shrine | Name = Yasaka Shrine | Image = [[File:IMG_tomosumi.JPG|250px]] | Location = {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|長崎県}}, {{ill|Shinkamigoto|ja|新上五島町}}, Tomozumigo, Tachiki 353 | Coordinates = {{Coord|32|59|56.0|N|129|10|32.7|E|type:landmark_region:JP-42|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined Deities = {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞男神}} (Main deity)
{{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主神}}, {{ill|Kanayamahiko|ja|金山彦神}}, {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神}}, {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神}} (Associated deities) | Shrine Rank = Former unranked shrine | Founded = Unknown | Annual Festival = November 3 | Rituals = Arikawa Kagura (Annual Grand Festival) | Map = {{Location map|Japan Nagasaki Prefecture|label=Yasaka Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }} '''Yasaka Shrine''' (八坂神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Shinkamigoto|ja|新上五島町}}, Nagasaki Prefecture, Japan. The shrine is situated near the summit of a small hill in Tomozumigo, with a lower shrine located in the residential area at the foot of the hill. == Enshrined Deities == The principal deity is {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞男神}}. The following deities are also enshrined: * {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主神}} (from {{ill|Kotohira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅神社}}) * {{ill|Kanayamahiko|ja|金山彦神}} (from Kotohira Shrine) * {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神}} (from {{ill|Shirahime Shrine|ja|白峯神社}}) * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神}} (from {{ill|Ōyama Shrine|ja|大山神社}}) == History == The exact date of the shrine's founding and its origins are unknown. In May 1874 (Meiji 7), the shrine was officially recognized as an unranked shrine. On December 4, 1908 (Meiji 41), {{ill|Kotohira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅神社}} was merged into Yasaka Shrine. Later, Shirahime Shrine and Ōyama Shrine were also merged, though the exact dates are unclear. == Festivals == During the annual festival on November 2 and 3, the {{ill|Arikawa Kagura|ja|有川神楽}} is performed. This kagura is part of the nationally designated {{ill|Gojima Kagura|ja|五島神楽}}, recognized as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property. ;Major Festivals and Rituals * {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭}} (February 18) * {{ill|Reitaisai|ja|例大祭}} (November 2–3) * {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭}} (November 29) == Sub-Shrines == * {{ill|Kamakura Shrine|ja|鎌倉神社}} * {{ill|Kotohira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅神社}} File:IMG_tomosumi2.JPG|Kotohira Shrine File:IMG_tomosumi3.JPG|Kamakura Shrine (sub-shrine) == Other Shrines in Tomozumigo == Other shrines in Tomozumigo include: * {{ill|Ebisu Shrine|ja|恵比須神社}} * {{ill|Gyōja Mountain Shrine|ja|行者山社}} Nearby shrines in adjacent areas: * {{ill|Harami Shrine|ja|孕神社}} (in Akao-go) * {{ill|Enohama Shrine|ja|江ノ濱神社}} (in Enohama-go) File:IMG_tomosumi4.JPG|Gyōja Mountain Shrine File:IMG_harami.JPG|Harami Shrine File:IMG_enohama.JPG|Enohama Shrine (Enohama-go) == References == * "Arikawa Town Local History" (平成6年), Arikawa Town Local History Compilation Committee * "Gojima Shrine Records" (昭和18年, Reprint Edition), Jingikai Minamimatsuura County {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Yasaka Shrines]] [[Category:Shrines in Shinkamigoto]] [[Category:Gion Shrines]] [[Category:Former unranked shrines in Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[ja:八坂神社 (新上五島町友住郷)]] [[Category:Yasaka Shrines]] [[Category:qq]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Yasaka Shrine (Tomozumigo, Shinkamigoto Town)}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:新上五島町の神社]] [[Category:祇園神社]] [[Category:長崎県の旧無格社]] [[ja:八坂神社 (新上五島町友住郷)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:八坂神社 (新上五島町友住郷)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:File]][[Category:File]] [[Category:unsorted completion categories]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]]

{{Shinto Shrine | Name = Yasaka Shrine | Image = [[File:IMG_tomosumi.JPG|250px]] | Location = {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|長崎県}}, {{ill|Shinkamigoto|ja|新上五島町}}, Tomozumigo, Tachiki 353 | Coordinates = {{Coord|32|59|56.0|N|129|10|32.7|E|type:landmark_region:JP-42|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined Deities = {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞男神}} (Main deity)
{{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主神}}, {{ill|Kanayamahiko|ja|金山彦神}}, {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神}}, {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神}} (Associated deities) | Shrine Rank = Former unranked shrine | Founded = Unknown | Annual Festival = November 3 | Rituals = Arikawa Kagura (Annual Grand Festival) | Map = {{Location map|Japan Nagasaki Prefecture|label=Yasaka Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }} '''Yasaka Shrine''' (八坂神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Shinkamigoto|ja|新上五島町}}, Nagasaki Prefecture, Japan. The shrine is situated near the summit of a small hill in Tomozumigo, with a lower shrine located in the residential area at the foot of the hill. == Enshrined Deities == The principal deity is {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞男神}}. The following deities are also enshrined: * {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主神}} (from {{ill|Kotohira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅神社}}) * {{ill|Kanayamahiko|ja|金山彦神}} (from Kotohira Shrine) * {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神}} (from {{ill|Shirahime Shrine|ja|白峯神社}}) * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神}} (from {{ill|Ōyama Shrine|ja|大山神社}}) == History == The exact date of the shrine's founding and its origins are unknown. In May 1874 (Meiji 7), the shrine was officially recognized as an unranked shrine. On December 4, 1908 (Meiji 41), {{ill|Kotohira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅神社}} was merged into Yasaka Shrine. Later, Shirahime Shrine and Ōyama Shrine were also merged, though the exact dates are unclear. == Festivals == During the annual festival on November 2 and 3, the {{ill|Arikawa Kagura|ja|有川神楽}} is performed. The shrine is situated near the summit of a small hill in Tomozumigo, with a lower shrine located in the residential area at the foot of the hill. == Enshrined Deities == The principal deity is {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞男神}}. The following deities are also enshrined: * {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主神}} (from {{ill|Kotohira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅神社}}) * {{ill|Kanayamahiko|ja|金山彦神}} (from Kotohira Shrine) * {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神}} (from {{ill|Shirahime Shrine|ja|白峯神社}}) * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神}} (from {{ill|Ōyama Shrine|ja|大山神社}}) == History == The exact date of the shrine's founding and its origins are unknown. In May 1874 (Meiji 7), the shrine was officially recognized as an unranked shrine. On December 4, 1908 (Meiji 41), {{ill|Kotohira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅神社}} was merged into Yasaka Shrine. Later, Shirahime Shrine and Ōyama Shrine were also merged, though the exact dates are unclear. == Festivals == During the annual festival on November 2 and 3, the {{ill|Arikawa Kagura|ja|有川神楽}} is performed. This kagura is part of the nationally designated {{ill|Gojima Kagura|ja|五島神楽}}, recognized as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property. ;Major Festivals and Rituals * {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭}} (February 18) * {{ill|Reitaisai|ja|例大祭}} (November 2–3) * {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭}} (November 29) == Sub-Shrines == * {{ill|Kamakura Shrine|ja|鎌倉神社}} * {{ill|Kotohira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅神社}} {{Shinto Shrine | Name = Yasaka Shrine | image = [[File:IMG_tomosumi.JPG|250px]] | location = {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|長崎県}}, {{ill|Shinkamigoto|ja|新上五島町}}, Tomozumigo, Tachiki 353 | coordinates = {{Coord|32|59|56.0|N|129|10|32.7|E|type:landmark_region:JP-42|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined Deities = {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞男神}} (Main deity)
{{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主神}}, {{ill|Kanayamahiko|ja|金山彦神}}, {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神}}, {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神}} (Associated deities) | type = Former unranked shrine | Founded = Unknown | festival = November 3 | Rituals = Arikawa Kagura (Annual Grand Festival) | Map = {{location map|Japan Nagasaki Prefecture|label=Yasaka Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }} '''Yasaka Shrine''' (八坂神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Shinkamigoto|ja|新上五島町}}, Nagasaki Prefecture, Japan. The shrine is situated near the summit of a small hill in Tomozumigo, with a lower shrine located in the residential area at the foot of the hill. == Enshrined Deities == The principal deity is {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞男神}}. The following deities are also enshrined: * {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主神}} (from {{ill|Kotohira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅神社}}) * {{ill|Kanayamahiko|ja|金山彦神}} (from Kotohira Shrine) * {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神}} (from {{ill|Shirahime Shrine|ja|白峯神社}}) * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神}} (from {{ill|Ōyama Shrine|ja|大山神社}}) == History == The exact date of the shrine's founding and its origins are unknown. In May 1874 (Meiji 7), the shrine was officially recognized as an unranked shrine. On December 4, 1908 (Meiji 41), {{ill|Kotohira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅神社}} was merged into Yasaka Shrine. Later, Shirahime Shrine and Ōyama Shrine were also merged, though the exact dates are unclear. == Festivals == During the annual festival on November 2 and 3, the {{ill|Arikawa Kagura|ja|有川神楽}} is performed. This kagura is part of the nationally designated {{ill|Gojima Kagura|ja|五島神楽}}, recognized as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property. ;Major Festivals and Rituals * {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭}} (February 18) * {{ill|Reitaisai|ja|例大祭}} (November 2–3) * {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭}} (November 29) == Sub-Shrines == * {{ill|Kamakura Shrine|ja|鎌倉神社}} * {{ill|Kotohira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅神社}} File:IMG_tomosumi2.JPG|Kotohira Shrine File:IMG_tomosumi3.JPG|Kamakura Shrine (sub-shrine) == Other Shrines in Tomozumigo == Other shrines in Tomozumigo include: * {{ill|Ebisu Shrine|ja|恵比須神社}} * The shrine is situated near the summit of a small hill in Tomozumigo, with a lower shrine located in the residential area at the foot of the hill. == Enshrined Deities == The principal deity is {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞男神}}. The following deities are also enshrined: * {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主神}} (from {{ill|Kotohira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅神社}}) * {{ill|Kanayamahiko|ja|金山彦神}} (from Kotohira Shrine) * {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神}} (from {{ill|Shirahime Shrine|ja|白峯神社}}) * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神}} (from {{ill|Ōyama Shrine|ja|大山神社}}) == History == The exact date of the shrine's founding and its origins are unknown. In May 1874 (Meiji 7), the shrine was officially recognized as an unranked shrine. On December 4, 1908 (Meiji 41), {{ill|Kotohira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅神社}} was merged into Yasaka Shrine. Later, Shirahime Shrine and Ōyama Shrine were also merged, though the exact dates are unclear. == Festivals == During the annual festival on November 2 and 3, the {{ill|Arikawa Kagura|ja|有川神楽}} is performed. This kagura is part of the nationally designated {{ill|Gojima Kagura|ja|五島神楽}}, recognized as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property. ;Major Festivals and Rituals * {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭}} (February 18) * {{ill|Reitaisai|ja|例大祭}} (November 2–3) * {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭}} (November 29) == Sub-Shrines == * {{ill|Kamakura Shrine|ja|鎌倉神社}} * {{ill|Kotohira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅神社}} File:IMG_tomosumi2.JPG|Kotohira Shrine File:IMG_tomosumi3.JPG|Kamakura Shrine (sub-shrine) == Other Shrines in Tomozumigo == Other shrines in Tomozumigo include: * {{ill|Ebisu Shrine|ja|恵比須神社}} * {{ill|Gyōja Mountain Shrine|ja|行者山社}} Nearby shrines in adjacent areas: * {{ill|Harami Shrine|ja|孕神社}} (in Akao-go) * {{ill|Enohama Shrine|ja|江ノ濱神社}} (in Enohama-go) File:IMG_tomosumi4.JPG|Gyōja Mountain Shrine File:IMG_harami.JPG|Harami Shrine File:IMG_enohama.JPG|Enohama Shrine (Enohama-go) == References == * "Arikawa Town Local History" (平成6年), Arikawa Town Local History Compilation Committee * "Gojima Shrine Records" (昭和18年, Reprint Edition), Jingikai Minamimatsuura County {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Yasaka Shrines]] [[Category:Shrines in Shinkamigoto]] [[Category:Gion Shrines]] {{Shinto Shrine | Name = Yasaka Shrine | image = [[File:IMG_tomosumi.JPG|250px]] | location = {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|長崎県}}, {{ill|Shinkamigoto|ja|新上五島町}}, Tomozumigo, Tachiki 353 | coordinates = {{Coord|32|59|56.0|N|129|10|32.7|E|type:landmark_region:JP-42|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined Deities = {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞男神}} (Main deity)
{{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主神}}, {{ill|Kanayamahiko|ja|金山彦神}}, {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神}}, {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神}} (Associated deities) | type = Former unranked shrine | Founded = Unknown | festival = November 3 | Rituals = Arikawa Kagura (Annual Grand Festival) | Map = {{location map|Japan Nagasaki Prefecture|label=Yasaka Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }} '''Yasaka Shrine''' (八坂神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Shinkamigoto|ja|新上五島町}}, Nagasaki Prefecture, Japan. The shrine is situated near the summit of a small hill in Tomozumigo, with a lower shrine located in the residential area at the foot of the hill. == Enshrined Deities == The principal deity is {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞男神}}. The following deities are also enshrined: * {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主神}} (from {{ill|Kotohira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅神社}}) * {{ill|Kanayamahiko|ja|金山彦神}} (from Kotohira Shrine) * {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神}} (from {{ill|Shirahime Shrine|ja|白峯神社}}) * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神}} (from {{ill|Ōyama Shrine|ja|大山神社}}) == History == The exact date of the shrine's founding and its origins are unknown. In May 1874 (Meiji 7), the shrine was officially recognized as an unranked shrine. On December 4, 1908 (Meiji 41), {{ill|Kotohira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅神社}} was merged into Yasaka Shrine. Later, Shirahime Shrine and Ōyama Shrine were also merged, though the exact dates are unclear. == Festivals == During the annual festival on November 2 and 3, the {{ill|Arikawa Kagura|ja|有川神楽}} is performed. This kagura is part of the nationally designated {{ill|Gojima Kagura|ja|五島神楽}}, recognized as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property. ;Major Festivals and Rituals * {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭}} (February 18) * {{ill|Reitaisai|ja|例大祭}} (November 2–3) * {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭}} (November 29) == Sub-Shrines == * {{ill|Kamakura Shrine|ja|鎌倉神社}} * {{ill|Kotohira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅神社}} File:IMG_tomosumi2.JPG|Kotohira Shrine File:IMG_tomosumi3.JPG|Kamakura Shrine (sub-shrine) == Other Shrines in Tomozumigo == Other shrines in Tomozumigo include: * {{ill|Ebisu Shrine|ja|恵比須神社}} * {{ill|Gyōja Mountain Shrine|ja|行者山社}} Nearby shrines in adjacent areas: * {{ill|Harami Shrine|ja|孕神社}} (in Akao-go) * {{ill|Enohama Shrine|ja|江ノ濱神社}} (in Enohama-go) File:IMG_tomosumi4.JPG|Gyōja Mountain Shrine File:IMG_harami.JPG|Harami Shrine File:IMG_enohama.JPG|Enohama Shrine (Enohama-go) == References == * "Arikawa Town Local History" (平成6年), Arikawa Town Local History Compilation Committee * "Gojima Shrine Records" (昭和18年, Reprint Edition), Jingikai Minamimatsuura County {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Yasaka Shrines]] [[Category:Shrines in Shinkamigoto]] [[Category:Gion Shrines]] {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主神}}, {{ill|Kanayamahiko|ja|金山彦神}}, {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神}}, {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神}} (Associated deities) | type = Former unranked shrine | Founded = Unknown | festival = November 3 | Rituals = Arikawa Kagura (Annual Grand Festival) | Map = {{location map|Japan Nagasaki Prefecture|label=Yasaka Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }} '''Yasaka Shrine''' (八坂神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Shinkamigoto|ja|新上五島町}}, Nagasaki Prefecture, Japan. The shrine is situated near the summit of a small hill in Tomozumigo, with a lower shrine located in the residential area at the foot of the hill. == Enshrined Deities == The principal deity is {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞男神}}. The following deities are also enshrined: * {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主神}} (from {{ill|Kotohira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅神社}}) * {{ill|Kanayamahiko|ja|金山彦神}} (from Kotohira Shrine) * {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神}} (from {{ill|Shirahime Shrine|ja|白峯神社}}) * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神}} (from {{ill|Ōyama Shrine|ja|大山神社}}) == History == The exact date of the shrine's founding and its origins are unknown. In May 1874 (Meiji 7), the shrine was officially recognized as an unranked shrine. On December 4, 1908 (Meiji 41), {{ill|Kotohira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅神社}} was merged into Yasaka Shrine. Later, Shirahime Shrine and Ōyama Shrine were also merged, though the exact dates are unclear. == Festivals == During the annual festival on November 2 and 3, the {{ill|Arikawa Kagura|ja|有川神楽}} is performed. This kagura is part of the nationally designated {{ill|Gojima Kagura|ja|五島神楽}}, recognized as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property. ;Major Festivals and Rituals * {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭}} (February 18) * {{ill|Reitaisai|ja|例大祭}} (November 2–3) * {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭}} (November 29) == Sub-Shrines == * {{ill|Kamakura Shrine|ja|鎌倉神社}} * {{ill|Kotohira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅神社}} File:IMG_tomosumi2.JPG|Kotohira Shrine File:IMG_tomosumi3.JPG|Kamakura Shrine (sub-shrine) == Other Shrines in Tomozumigo == Other shrines in Tomozumigo include: * {{ill|Ebisu Shrine|ja|恵比須神社}} * {{ill|Gyōja Mountain Shrine|ja|行者山社}} Nearby shrines in adjacent areas: * {{ill|Harami Shrine|ja|孕神社}} (in Akao-go) * {{ill|Enohama Shrine|ja|江ノ濱神社}} (in Enohama-go) File:IMG_tomosumi4.JPG|Gyōja Mountain Shrine File:IMG_harami.JPG|Harami Shrine File:IMG_enohama.JPG|Enohama Shrine (Enohama-go) == References == * "Arikawa Town Local History" (平成6年), Arikawa Town Local History Compilation Committee * "Gojima Shrine Records" (昭和18年, Reprint Edition), Jingikai Minamimatsuura County {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Yasaka Shrines]] [[Category:Shrines in Shinkamigoto]] [[Category:Gion Shrines]] {{Shinto Shrine | Name = Yasaka Shrine | image = [[File:IMG_tomosumi.JPG|250px]] | location = {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|長崎県}}, {{ill|Shinkamigoto|ja|新上五島町}}, Tomozumigo, Tachiki 353 | coordinates = {{Coord|32|59|56.0|N|129|10|32.7|E|type:landmark_region:JP-42|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined Deities = {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞男神}} (Main deity)
{{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主神}}, {{ill|Kanayamahiko|ja|金山彦神}}, {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神}}, {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神}} (Associated deities) | type = Former unranked shrine | Founded = Unknown | festival = November 3 | Rituals = Arikawa Kagura (Annual Grand Festival) | Map = {{location map|Japan Nagasaki Prefecture|label=Yasaka Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }} '''Yasaka Shrine''' (八坂神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Shinkamigoto|ja|新上五島町}}, Nagasaki Prefecture, Japan. The shrine is situated near the summit of a small hill in Tomozumigo, with a lower shrine located in the residential area at the foot of the hill. == Enshrined Deities == The principal deity is {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞男神}}. The following deities are also enshrined: * {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主神}} (from {{ill|Kotohira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅神社}}) * {{ill|Kanayamahiko|ja|金山彦神}} (from Kotohira Shrine) * {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神}} (from {{ill|Shirahime Shrine|ja|白峯神社}}) * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神}} (from {{ill|Ōyama Shrine|ja|大山神社}}) == History == The exact date of the shrine's founding and its origins are unknown. In May 1874 (Meiji 7), the shrine was officially recognized as an unranked shrine. On December 4, 1908 (Meiji 41), {{ill|Kotohira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅神社}} was merged into Yasaka Shrine. Later, Shirahime Shrine and Ōyama Shrine were also merged, though the exact dates are unclear. == Festivals == During the annual festival on November 2 and 3, the {{ill|Arikawa Kagura|ja|有川神楽}} is performed. This kagura is part of the nationally designated {{ill|Gojima Kagura|ja|五島神楽}}, recognized as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property. ;Major Festivals and Rituals * {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭}} (February 18) * {{ill|Reitaisai|ja|例大祭}} (November 2–3) * {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭}} (November 29) == Sub-Shrines == * {{ill|Kamakura Shrine|ja|鎌倉神社}} * {{ill|Kotohira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅神社}} File:IMG_tomosumi2.JPG|Kotohira Shrine File:IMG_tomosumi3.JPG|Kamakura Shrine (sub-shrine) == Other Shrines in Tomozumigo == Other shrines in Tomozumigo include: * {{ill|Ebisu Shrine|ja|恵比須神社}} * {{ill|Gyōja Mountain Shrine|ja|行者山社}} Nearby shrines in adjacent areas: * {{ill|Harami Shrine|ja|孕神社}} (in Akao-go) * {{ill|Enohama Shrine|ja|江ノ濱神社}} (in Enohama-go) File:IMG_tomosumi4.JPG|Gyōja Mountain Shrine File:IMG_harami.JPG|Harami Shrine File:IMG_enohama.JPG|Enohama Shrine (Enohama-go) == References == * "Arikawa Town Local History" (平成6年), Arikawa Town Local History Compilation Committee * "Gojima Shrine Records" (昭和18年, Reprint Edition), Jingikai Minamimatsuura County {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Yasaka Shrines]] [[Category:Shrines in Shinkamigoto]] [[Category:Gion Shrines]] {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主神}}, {{ill|Kanayamahiko|ja|金山彦神}}, {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神}}, {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神}} (Associated deities) | type = Former unranked shrine | Founded = Unknown | festival = November 3 | Rituals = Arikawa Kagura (Annual Grand Festival) | Map = {{location map|Japan Nagasaki Prefecture|label=Yasaka Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }} '''Yasaka Shrine''' (八坂神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Shinkamigoto|ja|新上五島町}}, Nagasaki Prefecture, Japan. The shrine is situated near the summit of a small hill in Tomozumigo, with a lower shrine located in the residential area at the foot of the hill. == Enshrined Deities == The principal deity is {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞男神}}. The following deities are also enshrined: * {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主神}} (from {{ill|Kotohira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅神社}}) * {{ill|Kanayamahiko|ja|金山彦神}} (from Kotohira Shrine) * {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神}} (from {{ill|Shirahime Shrine|ja|白峯神社}}) * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神}} (from {{ill|Ōyama Shrine|ja|大山神社}}) == History == The exact date of the shrine's founding and its origins are unknown. In May 1874 (Meiji 7), the shrine was officially recognized as an unranked shrine. On December 4, 1908 (Meiji 41), {{ill|Kotohira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅神社}} was merged into Yasaka Shrine. Later, Shirahime Shrine and Ōyama Shrine were also merged, though the exact dates are unclear. == Festivals == During the annual festival on November 2 and 3, the {{ill|Arikawa Kagura|ja|有川神楽}} is performed. This kagura is part of the nationally designated {{ill|Gojima Kagura|ja|五島神楽}}, recognized as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property. ;Major Festivals and Rituals * {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭}} (February 18) * {{ill|Reitaisai|ja|例大祭}} (November 2–3) * {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭}} (November 29) == Sub-Shrines == * {{ill|Kamakura Shrine|ja|鎌倉神社}} * {{ill|Kotohira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅神社}} File:IMG_tomosumi2.JPG|Kotohira Shrine File:IMG_tomosumi3.JPG|Kamakura Shrine (sub-shrine) == Other Shrines in Tomozumigo == Other shrines in Tomozumigo include: * {{ill|Ebisu Shrine|ja|恵比須神社}} * {{ill|Gyōja Mountain Shrine|ja|行者山社}} Nearby shrines in adjacent areas: * {{ill|Harami Shrine|ja|孕神社}} (in Akao-go) * {{ill|Enohama Shrine|ja|江ノ濱神社}} (in Enohama-go) File:IMG_tomosumi4.JPG|Gyōja Mountain Shrine File:IMG_harami.JPG|Harami Shrine File:IMG_enohama.JPG|Enohama Shrine (Enohama-go) == References == * "Arikawa Town Local History" (平成6年), Arikawa Town Local History Compilation {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主神}}, {{ill|Kanayamahiko|ja|金山彦神}}, {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神}}, {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神}} (Associated deities) | type = Former unranked shrine | Founded = Unknown | festival = November 3 | Rituals = Arikawa Kagura (Annual Grand Festival) | Map = {{location map|Japan Nagasaki Prefecture|label=Yasaka Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }} '''Yasaka Shrine''' (八坂神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Shinkamigoto|ja|新上五島町}}, Nagasaki Prefecture, Japan. The shrine is situated near the summit of a small hill in Tomozumigo, with a lower shrine located in the residential area at the foot of the hill. == Enshrined Deities == The principal deity is {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞男神}}. The following deities are also enshrined: * {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主神}} (from {{ill|Kotohira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅神社}}) * {{ill|Kanayamahiko|ja|金山彦神}} (from Kotohira Shrine) * {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神}} (from {{ill|Shirahime Shrine|ja|白峯神社}}) * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神}} (from {{ill|Ōyama Shrine|ja|大山神社}}) == History == The exact date of the shrine's founding and its origins are unknown. In May 1874 (Meiji 7), the shrine was officially recognized as an unranked shrine. On December 4, 1908 (Meiji 41), {{ill|Kotohira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅神社}} was merged into Yasaka Shrine. Later, Shirahime Shrine and Ōyama Shrine were also merged, though the exact dates are unclear. == Festivals == During the annual festival on November 2 and 3, the {{ill|Arikawa Kagura|ja|有川神楽}} is performed. This kagura is part of the nationally designated {{ill|Gojima Kagura|ja|五島神楽}}, recognized as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property. ;Major Festivals and Rituals * {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭}} (February 18) * {{ill|Reitaisai|ja|例大祭}} (November 2–3) * {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭}} (November 29) == Sub-Shrines == * {{ill|Kamakura Shrine|ja|鎌倉神社}} * {{ill|Kotohira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅神社}} {{ill|Gyōja Mountain Shrine|ja|行者山社}} Nearby shrines in adjacent areas: * {{ill|Harami Shrine|ja|孕神社}} (in Akao-go) * {{ill|Enohama Shrine|ja|江ノ濱神社}} (in Enohama-go) File:IMG_tomosumi4.JPG|Gyōja {{Shinto Shrine | Name = Yasaka Shrine | image = [[File:IMG_tomosumi.JPG|250px]] | location = {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|長崎県}}, {{ill|Shinkamigoto|ja|新上五島町}}, Tomozumigo, Tachiki 353 | coordinates = {{Coord|32|59|56.0|N|129|10|32.7|E|type:landmark_region:JP-42|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined Deities = {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞男神}} (Main deity)
{{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主神}}, {{ill|Kanayamahiko|ja|金山彦神}}, {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神}}, {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神}} (Associated deities) | type = Former unranked shrine | Founded = Unknown | festival = November 3 | Rituals = Arikawa Kagura (Annual Grand Festival) | Map = {{location map|Japan Nagasaki Prefecture|label=Yasaka Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }} '''Yasaka Shrine''' (八坂神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Shinkamigoto|ja|新上五島町}}, Nagasaki Prefecture, Japan. The shrine is situated near the summit of a small hill in Tomozumigo, with a lower shrine located in the residential area at the foot of the hill. == Enshrined Deities == The principal deity is {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞男神}}. The following deities are also enshrined: * {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主神}} (from {{ill|Kotohira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅神社}}) * {{ill|Kanayamahiko|ja|金山彦神}} (from Kotohira Shrine) * {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神}} (from {{ill|Shirahime Shrine|ja|白峯神社}}) * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神}} (from {{ill|Ōyama Shrine|ja|大山神社}}) == History == The exact date of the shrine's founding and its origins are unknown. In May 1874 (Meiji 7), the shrine was officially recognized as an unranked shrine. On December 4, 1908 (Meiji 41), {{ill|Kotohira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅神社}} was merged into Yasaka Shrine. Later, Shirahime Shrine and Ōyama Shrine were also merged, though the exact dates are unclear. == Festivals == During the annual festival on November 2 and 3, the {{ill|Arikawa Kagura|ja|有川神楽}} is performed. This kagura is part of the nationally designated {{ill|Gojima Kagura|ja|五島神楽}}, recognized as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property. ;Major Festivals and Rituals * {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭}} (February 18) * {{ill|Reitaisai|ja|例大祭}} (November 2–3) * {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭}} (November 29) == Sub-Shrines == * {{ill|Kamakura Shrine|ja|鎌倉神社}} * {{ill|Kotohira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅神社}} File:IMG_tomosumi2.JPG|Kotohira Shrine File:IMG_tomosumi3.JPG|Kamakura Shrine (sub-shrine) == Other Shrines in Tomozumigo == Other shrines in Tomozumigo include: * {{ill|Ebisu Shrine|ja|恵比須神社}} * {{ill|Gyōja Mountain Shrine|ja|行者山社}} Nearby shrines in adjacent areas: * {{ill|Harami Shrine|ja|孕神社}} (in Akao-go) * {{ill|Enohama Shrine|ja|江ノ濱神社}} (in Enohama-go) File:IMG_tomosumi4.JPG|Gyōja Mountain Shrine File:IMG_harami.JPG|Harami Shrine File:IMG_enohama.JPG|Enohama Shrine (Enohama-go) == References == * "Arikawa Town Local History" (平成6年), Arikawa Town Local History Compilation Committee * "Gojima Shrine Records" (昭和18年, Reprint Edition), Jingikai Minamimatsuura County {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Yasaka Shrines]] [[Category:Shrines in Shinkamigoto]] [[Category:Gion Shrines]] {{Shinto Shrine | Name = Yasaka Shrine | image = [[File:IMG_tomosumi.JPG|250px]] | location = {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|長崎県}}, {{ill|Shinkamigoto|ja|新上五島町}}, Tomozumigo, Tachiki 353 | coordinates = {{Coord|32|59|56.0|N|129|10|32.7|E|type:landmark_region:JP-42|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined Deities = {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞男神}} (Main deity)
{{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主神}}, {{ill|Kanayamahiko|ja|金山彦神}}, {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神}}, {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神}} (Associated deities) | type = Former unranked shrine | Founded = Unknown | festival = November 3 | Rituals = Arikawa Kagura (Annual Grand Festival) | Map = {{location map|Japan Nagasaki Prefecture|label=Yasaka Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }} '''Yasaka Shrine''' (八坂神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Shinkamigoto|ja|新上五島町}}, Nagasaki Prefecture, Japan. The shrine is situated near the summit of a small hill in Tomozumigo, with a lower shrine located in the residential area at the foot of the hill. == Enshrined Deities == The principal deity is {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞男神}}. The following deities are also enshrined: * {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主神}} (from {{ill|Kotohira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅神社}}) * {{ill|Kanayamahiko|ja|金山彦神}} (from Kotohira Shrine) * {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神}} (from {{ill|Shirahime Shrine|ja|白峯神社}}) * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神}} (from {{ill|Ōyama Shrine|ja|大山神社}}) == History == The exact date of the shrine's founding and its origins are unknown. In May 1874 (Meiji 7), the shrine was officially recognized as an unranked shrine. On December 4, 1908 (Meiji 41), {{ill|Kotohira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅神社}} was merged into Yasaka Shrine. Later, Shirahime Shrine and Ōyama Shrine were also merged, though the exact dates are unclear. == Festivals == During the annual festival on November 2 and 3, the {{ill|Arikawa Kagura|ja|有川神楽}} is performed. This kagura is part of the nationally designated {{ill|Gojima Kagura|ja|五島神楽}}, recognized as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property. ;Major Festivals and Rituals * {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭}} (February 18) * {{ill|Reitaisai|ja|例大祭}} (November 2–3) * {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭}} (November 29) == Sub-Shrines == * {{ill|Kamakura Shrine|ja|鎌倉神社}} * {{ill|Kotohira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅神社}} File:IMG_tomosumi2.JPG|Kotohira Shrine File:IMG_tomosumi3.JPG|Kamakura Shrine (sub-shrine) == Other Shrines in Tomozumigo == Other shrines in Tomozumigo include: * {{ill|Ebisu Shrine|ja|恵比須神社}} * {{ill|Gyōja Mountain Shrine|ja|行者山社}} Nearby shrines in adjacent areas: * {{ill|Harami Shrine|ja|孕神社}} (in Akao-go) * {{ill|Enohama Shrine|ja|江ノ濱神社}} (in Enohama-go) File:IMG_tomosumi4.JPG|Gyōja Mountain Shrine File:IMG_harami.JPG|Harami Shrine File:IMG_enohama.JPG|Enohama Shrine (Enohama-go) == References == * "Arikawa Town Local History" (平成6年), Arikawa Town Local History Compilation {{Shinto Shrine | Name = Yasaka Shrine | image = [[File:IMG_tomosumi.JPG|250px]] | location = {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|長崎県}}, {{ill|Shinkamigoto|ja|新上五島町}}, Tomozumigo, Tachiki 353 | coordinates = {{Coord|32|59|56.0|N|129|10|32.7|E|type:landmark_region:JP-42|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined Deities = {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞男神}} (Main deity)
{{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主神}}, {{ill|Kanayamahiko|ja|金山彦神}}, {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神}}, {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神}} (Associated deities) | type = Former unranked shrine | Founded = Unknown | festival = November 3 | Rituals = Arikawa Kagura (Annual Grand Festival) | Map = {{location map|Japan Nagasaki Prefecture|label=Yasaka Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }} '''Yasaka Shrine''' (八坂神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Shinkamigoto|ja|新上五島町}}, Nagasaki Prefecture, Japan. The shrine is situated near the summit of a small hill in Tomozumigo, with a lower shrine located in the residential area at the foot of the hill. == Enshrined Deities == The principal deity is {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞男神}}. The following deities are also enshrined: * {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主神}} (from {{ill|Kotohira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅神社}}) * {{ill|Kanayamahiko|ja|金山彦神}} (from Kotohira Shrine) * {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神}} (from {{ill|Shirahime Shrine|ja|白峯神社}}) * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神}} (from {{ill|Ōyama Shrine|ja|大山神社}}) == History == The exact date of the shrine's founding and its origins are unknown. In May 1874 (Meiji 7), the shrine was officially recognized as an unranked shrine. On December 4, 1908 (Meiji 41), {{ill|Kotohira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅神社}} was merged into Yasaka Shrine. Later, Shirahime Shrine and Ōyama Shrine were also merged, though the exact dates are unclear. == Festivals == During the annual festival on November 2 and 3, the {{ill|Arikawa Kagura|ja|有川神楽}} is performed. This kagura is part of the nationally designated {{ill|Gojima Kagura|ja|五島神楽}}, recognized as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property. ;Major Festivals and Rituals * {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭}} (February 18) * {{ill|Reitaisai|ja|例大祭}} (November 2–3) * {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭}} (November 29) == Sub-Shrines == * {{ill|Kamakura Shrine|ja|鎌倉神社}} * {{ill|Kotohira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅神社}} File:IMG_tomosumi2.JPG|Kotohira Shrine File:IMG_tomosumi3.JPG|Kamakura Shrine (sub-shrine) == Other Shrines in Tomozumigo == Other shrines in Tomozumigo include: * {{ill|Ebisu Shrine|ja|恵比須神社}} * {{ill|Gyōja Mountain Shrine|ja|行者山社}} Nearby shrines in adjacent areas: * {{ill|Harami Shrine|ja|孕神社}} (in Akao-go) * {{ill|Enohama Shrine|ja|江ノ濱神社}} (in Enohama-go) File:IMG_tomosumi4.JPG|Gyōja Mountain Shrine File:IMG_harami.JPG|Harami Shrine File:IMG_enohama.JPG|Enohama Shrine (Enohama-go) == References == * "Arikawa Town Local History" (平成6年), Arikawa Town Local History Compilation {{Shinto Shrine | Name = Yasaka Shrine | image = [[File:IMG_tomosumi.JPG|250px]] | location = {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|長崎県}}, {{ill|Shinkamigoto|ja|新上五島町}}, Tomozumigo, Tachiki 353 | coordinates = {{Coord|32|59|56.0|N|129|10|32.7|E|type:landmark_region:JP-42|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined Deities = {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞男神}} (Main deity)
{{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主神}}, {{ill|Kanayamahiko|ja|金山彦神}}, {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神}}, {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神}} (Associated deities) | type = Former unranked shrine | Founded = Unknown | festival = November 3 | Rituals = Arikawa Kagura (Annual Grand Festival) | Map = {{location map|Japan Nagasaki Prefecture|label=Yasaka Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }} '''Yasaka Shrine''' (八坂神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Shinkamigoto|ja|新上五島町}}, Nagasaki Prefecture, Japan. The shrine is situated near the summit of a small hill in Tomozumigo, with a lower shrine located in the residential area at the foot of the hill. == Enshrined Deities == The principal deity is {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞男神}}. The following deities are also enshrined: * {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主神}} (from {{ill|Kotohira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅神社}}) * {{ill|Kanayamahiko|ja|金山彦神}} (from Kotohira Shrine) * {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神}} (from {{ill|Shirahime Shrine|ja|白峯神社}}) * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神}} (from {{ill|Ōyama Shrine|ja|大山神社}}) == History == The exact date of the shrine's founding and its origins are unknown. In May 1874 (Meiji 7), the shrine was officially recognized as an unranked shrine. On December 4, 1908 (Meiji 41), {{ill|Kotohira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅神社}} was merged into Yasaka Shrine. Later, Shirahime Shrine and Ōyama Shrine were also merged, though the exact dates are unclear. == Festivals == During the annual festival on November 2 and 3, the {{ill|Arikawa Kagura|ja|有川神楽}} is performed. This kagura is part of the nationally designated {{ill|Gojima Kagura|ja|五島神楽}}, recognized as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property. ;Major Festivals and Rituals * {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭}} (February 18) * {{ill|Reitaisai|ja|例大祭}} (November 2–3) * {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭}} (November 29) == Sub-Shrines == * {{ill|Kamakura Shrine|ja|鎌倉神社}} * {{ill|Kotohira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅神社}} File:IMG_tomosumi2.JPG|Kotohira Shrine File:IMG_tomosumi3.JPG|Kamakura Shrine (sub-shrine) == Other Shrines in Tomozumigo == Other shrines in Tomozumigo include: * {{ill|Ebisu Shrine|ja|恵比須神社}} * {{ill|Gyōja Mountain Shrine|ja|行者山社}} Nearby shrines in adjacent areas: * {{ill|Harami Shrine|ja|孕神社}} (in Akao-go) * {{ill|Enohama Shrine|ja|江ノ濱神社}} (in Enohama-go) File:IMG_tomosumi4.JPG|Gyōja Mountain Shrine File:IMG_harami.JPG|Harami Shrine File:IMG_enohama.JPG|Enohama Shrine (Enohama-go) == References == * "Arikawa Town Local History" (平成6年), Arikawa Town Local History Compilation Committee * "Gojima Shrine Records" (昭和18th year , Reprint Edition), Jingikai Minamimatsuura County {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Yasaka Shrines]] [[Category:Shrines in Shinkamigoto]] [[Category:Gion The shrine is situated near the summit of a small hill in Tomozumigo, with a lower shrine located in the residential area at the foot of the hill. == Enshrined Deities == The principal deity is {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞男神|12=draft}}. The following deities are also enshrined: * {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主神|12=draft}} (from {{ill|Kotohira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅神社|12=draft}}) * {{ill|Kanayamahiko|ja|金山彦神|12=draft}} (from Kotohira Shrine) * {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神|12=draft}} (from {{ill|Shirahime Shrine|ja|白峯神社|12=draft}}) * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神|12=draft}} (from {{ill|Ōyama Shrine|ja|大山神社|12=draft}}) == History == The exact date of the shrine's founding and its origins are unknown. In May 1874 (Meiji 7), the shrine was officially recognized as an unranked shrine. On December 4, 1908 (Meiji 41), {{ill|Kotohira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅神社|12=draft}} was merged into Yasaka Shrine. Later, Shirahime Shrine and Ōyama Shrine were also merged, though the exact dates are unclear. == Festivals == During the annual festival on November 2 and 3, the {{ill|Arikawa Kagura|ja|有川神楽|12=draft}} is performed. Later, Shirahime Shrine and Ōyama Shrine were also merged, though the exact dates are unclear. == Festivals == During the annual festival on November 2 and 3, the {{ill|Arikawa Kagura|ja|有川神楽|12=draft}} is performed. This kagura is part of the nationally designated {{ill|Gojima Kagura|ja|五島神楽|12=draft}}, recognized as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property. ;Major Festivals and Rituals * {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭|12=draft}} (February 18) * {{ill|Reitaisai|ja|例大祭|12=draft}} (November 2–3) * {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭|12=draft}} (November 29) == Sub-Shrines == * {{ill|Kamakura Shrine|ja|鎌倉神社|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kotohira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅神社|12=draft}} File:IMG_tomosumi2.JPG|Kotohira Shrine File:IMG_tomosumi3.JPG|Kamakura Shrine (sub-shrine) == Other Shrines in Tomozumigo == Other shrines in Tomozumigo include: * {{ill|Ebisu Shrine|ja|恵比須神社|12=draft}} * {{ill|Gyōja Mountain Shrine|ja|行者山社|12=draft}} Nearby shrines in adjacent areas: * {{ill|Harami Shrine|ja|孕神社|12=draft}} (in Akao-go) * {{ill|Enohama Shrine|ja|江ノ濱神社|12=draft}} (in Enohama-go) File:IMG_tomosumi4.JPG|Gyōja Mountain Shrine File:IMG_harami.JPG|Harami Shrine File:IMG_enohama.JPG|Enohama Shrine (Enohama-go) == References == * "Arikawa Town Local History" (平成6年), Arikawa Town Local History Compilation Committee * "Gojima Shrine Records" (昭和18th year , Reprint Edition), Jingikai Minamimatsuura County {{Shinto Shrine | Name = Yasaka Shrine | image = [[File:IMG_tomosumi.JPG|250px]] | location = {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|長崎県|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Shinkamigoto|ja|新上五島町|12=draft|12=draft}}, Tomozumigo, Tachiki 353 | coordinates = {{Coord|32|59|56.0|N|129|10|32.7|E|type:landmark_region:JP-42|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined Deities = {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞男神|12=draft|12=draft}} (Main deity)
{{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主神|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kanayamahiko|ja|金山彦神|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神|12=draft|12=draft}} (Associated deities) | type = Former unRanked shrine | Founded = Unknown | festival = November 3 | Rituals = Arikawa Kagura (Annual Grand Festival) | Map = {{location map|Japan Nagasaki Prefecture|label=Yasaka Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }} '''Yasaka Shrine''' (八坂神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Shinkamigoto|ja|新上五島町|12=draft|12=draft}}, Nagasaki Prefecture, Japan. The shrine is situated near the summit of a small hill in Tomozumigo, with a lower shrine located in the residential area at the foot of the hill. == Enshrined Deities == The principal deity is {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞男神|12=draft|12=draft}}. The following deities are also enshrined: * {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主神|12=draft|12=draft}} (from {{ill|Kotohira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅神社|12=draft|12=draft}}) * {{ill|Kanayamahiko|ja|金山彦神|12=draft|12=draft}} (from Kotohira Shrine) * {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神|12=draft|12=draft}} (from {{ill|Shirahime Shrine|ja|白峯神社|12=draft|12=draft}}) * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神|12=draft|12=draft}} (from {{ill|Ōyama Shrine|ja|大山神社|12=draft|12=draft}}) == History == The exact date of the shrine's founding and its origins are unknown. In May 1874 (Meiji 7), the shrine was officially recognized as an unRanked shrine. On December 4, 1908 (Meiji 41), {{ill|Kotohira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅神社|12=draft|12=draft}} was merged into Yasaka Shrine. Later, Shirahime Shrine and Ōyama Shrine were also merged, though the exact dates are unclear. == Festivals == During the annual festival on November 2 and 3, the {{ill|Arikawa Kagura|ja|有川神楽|12=draft|12=draft}} is performed. This kagura is part of the nationally designated {{ill|Gojima Kagura|ja|五島神楽|12=draft|12=draft}}, recognized as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property. ;Major Festivals and Rituals * {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭|12=draft|12=draft}} (February 18) * {{ill|Reitaisai|ja|例大祭|12=draft|12=draft}} (November 2–3) * {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭|12=draft|12=draft}} (November 29) == Sub-Shrines == * {{ill|Kamakura Shrine|ja|鎌倉神社|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kotohira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅神社|12=draft|12=draft}} File:IMG_tomosumi2.JPG|Kotohira Shrine File:IMG_tomosumi3.JPG|Kamakura Shrine (sub-shrine) == Other Shrines in Tomozumigo == Other shrines in Tomozumigo include: * {{ill|Ebisu Shrine|ja|恵比須神社|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Gyōja Mountain Shrine|ja|行者山社|12=draft|12=draft}} Nearby shrines in adjacent areas: * {{ill|Harami Shrine|ja|孕神社|12=draft|12=draft}} (in Akao-go) * {{ill|Enohama Shrine|ja|江ノ濱神社|12=draft|12=draft}} (in Enohama-go) File:IMG_tomosumi4.JPG|Gyōja Mountain Shrine File:IMG_harami.JPG|Harami Shrine File:IMG_enohama.JPG|Enohama Shrine (Enohama-go) == References == * "Arikawa Town Local History" (平成6年), Arikawa Town Local History Compilation Committee * "Gojima Shrine Records" (昭和18th year , Reprint Edition), Jingikai Minamimatsuura County {{Shinto-stub}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Yasaka Shrines]] [[Category:Shrines in Shinkamigoto]] [[Category:Gion Shrines]] [[Category:Former unRanked shrines in Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[Category:新上五島町の神社]] [[Category:Gion shrines]] [[Category:長崎県の旧無格社]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:八坂神社 (新上五島町友住郷)]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:orphaned pages as of April 8]] }} [[ja:八坂神社 (新上五島町友住郷)]] [[sn:Yasaka Shrine (Tomozumigo, Shinkamigoto Town)]] [[en:Yasaka Shrine (Tomozumigo, Shinkamigoto Town)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Yasaka Shrine (Tomozumigo, Shinkamigoto Town)}}

revision.comment: 14 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:八坂神社 (新上五島町友住郷) up to rev 97032503
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 八坂神社 |画像 = [[File:IMG_tomosumi.JPG|250px]] |所在地 = 長崎県南松浦郡新上五島町友住郷字立木353 |位置 = {{Coord|32|59|56.0|N|129|10|32.7|E|type:landmark_region:JP-42|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = 素盞男神 |社格 = 旧無格社 |創建 = 不詳 |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = 11月3日 |神事 = 有川神楽（例大祭） |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Nagasaki Prefecture|label=八坂神社|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }} '''八坂神社'''（やさかじんじゃ）は、[[長崎県]][[新上五島町]]友住郷に鎮座する[[神社]]である。同郷の小高い山の頂上付近に鎮座し、麓の住宅地には[[下社]]がある。
== 祭神 == [[スサノオ|素盞男神]]を主祭神に、[[大物主神]]、[[金山彦神]]、[[事代主神]]、 [[大山祇神]]の4柱を[[配祀]]する。
配祀神中の大物主神・金山彦神は金刀比羅神社の、事代主神は白峯神社の、大山祇神は大山神社の祭神である。
== 歴史 == [[ファイル:IMG_tomosumi1.JPG|180px|thumb|社殿]] 創建年代、及び由緒等は不詳。
[[明治]]7年（[[1874年]]）5月5日、[[無格社]]に列せられる。明治41年（[[1908年]]）12月4日、同郷鎮座の[[金刀比羅神社]]を[[合祀]]。また、年代は不詳だが同郷の白峯神社・大山神社も合祀された。
== 祭祀 == 11月2、3日の例祭には[[有川神楽]]が奉納される。有川神楽は国の[[選択無形民俗文化財]]にも選択された[[五島神楽]]に含まれる。
;主な祭礼・神事 :*[[祈年祭]]（2月18日） :*[[例大祭]]（11月2、3日） :*[[新嘗祭]]（11月29日）
{{clear}} == 境内社 == [[ファイル:IMG_tomosumi2.JPG|180px|thumb|金刀比羅神社]] [[ファイル:IMG_tomosumi3.JPG|180px|thumb|境内社・鎌倉神社]] *鎌倉神社
{{clear}}
== その他の神社 == その他の友住郷の神社に'''恵比須神社'''、'''行者山社'''がある。また、隣接する赤尾郷に'''[[孕神社]]'''が、江ノ浜郷に'''[[江ノ濱神社]]'''がある。 <gallery> File:IMG_tomosumi4.JPG|行者山社 File:IMG_harami.JPG|孕神社 File:IMG_enohama.JPG|江ノ濱神社（江ノ浜郷） </gallery>
== 参考文献 == *『[[有川町]]郷土誌 平成6年』有川町郷土史編纂委員会 *『五島神社誌 昭和18年（復刻版）』神祇会南松浦郡部著
{{shinto-stub}}
{{DEFAULTSORT:やさかしんしや}} [[Category:八坂神社]] [[Category:新上五島町の神社]] [[Category:祇園神社]] [[Category:長崎県の旧無格社]]

== その他の神社 == その他の友住郷の神社に'''恵比須神社'''、'''行者山社'''がある。また、隣接する赤尾郷に'''[[孕神社]]'''が、江ノ浜郷に'''[[江ノ濱神社]]'''がある。 <gallery> File:IMG_tomosumi4.JPG|行者山社 File:IMG_harami.JPG|孕神社 File:IMG_enohama.JPG|江ノ濱神社（江ノ浜郷） </gallery>
== 参考文献 == *『[[有川町]]郷土誌 平成6年』有川町郷土史編纂委員会 *『五島神社誌 昭和18年（復刻版）』神祇会南松浦郡部著

{{Shinto Shrine | Name = Yasaka Shrine | image = [[File:IMG_tomosumi.JPG|250px]] | location = {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|長崎県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Shinkamigoto|ja|新上五島町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Tomozumigo, Tachiki 353 | coordinates = {{Coord|32|59|56.0|N|129|10|32.7|E|type:landmark_region:JP-42|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined Deities = {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞男神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Main deity)
{{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kanayamahiko|ja|金山彦神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Associated deities) | type = Former unranked shrine | Founded = Unknown | festival = November 3 | Rituals = Arikawa Kagura (Annual Grand Festival) | Map = {{location map|Japan Nagasaki Prefecture|label=Yasaka Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }} '''Yasaka Shrine''' (八坂神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Shinkamigoto|ja|新上五島町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Nagasaki Prefecture, Japan. The shrine is situated near the summit of a small hill in Tomozumigo, with a lower shrine located in the residential area at the foot of the hill. == Enshrined Deities == The principal deity is {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞男神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The following deities are also enshrined: * {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (from {{ill|Kotohira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) * {{ill|Kanayamahiko|ja|金山彦神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (from Kotohira Shrine) * {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (from {{ill|Shirahime Shrine|ja|白峯神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (from {{ill|Ōyama Shrine|ja|大山神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) == History == The exact date of the shrine's founding and its origins are unknown. In May 1874 (Meiji 7), the shrine was officially recognized as an unranked shrine. On December 4, 1908 (Meiji 41), {{ill|Kotohira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} was merged into Yasaka Shrine. Later, Shirahime Shrine and Ōyama Shrine were also merged, though the exact dates are unclear. == Festivals == During the annual festival on November 2 and 3, the {{ill|Arikawa Kagura|ja|有川神楽|qq=draft|qq=draft}} is performed. This kagura is part of the nationally designated {{ill|Gojima Kagura|ja|五島神楽|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, recognized as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property. ;Major Festivals and Rituals * {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (February 18) * {{ill|Reitaisai|ja|例大祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (November 2–3) * {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (November 29) == Sub-Shrines == * {{ill|Kamakura Shrine|ja|鎌倉神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Kotohira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} File:IMG_tomosumi2.JPG|Kotohira Shrine File:IMG_tomosumi3.JPG|Kamakura Shrine (sub-shrine) == Other Shrines in Tomozumigo == Other shrines in Tomozumigo include: * {{ill|Ebisu Shrine|ja|恵比須神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Gyōja Mountain Shrine|ja|行者山社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Nearby shrines in adjacent areas: * {{ill|Harami Shrine|ja|孕神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (in Akao-go) * {{ill|Enohama Shrine|ja|江ノ濱神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (in Enohama-go) File:IMG_tomosumi4.JPG|Gyōja Mountain Shrine File:IMG_harami.JPG|Harami Shrine File:IMG_enohama.JPG|Enohama Shrine (Enohama-go) == References == * "Arikawa Town Local History" (平成6年), Arikawa Town Local History Compilation Committee * "Gojima Shrine Records" (昭和18th year , Reprint Edition), Jingikai Minamimatsuura County {{Shinto-stub}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Yasaka Shrines]] [[Category:Shrines in Shinkamigoto]] [[Category:Gion Shrines]] [[Category:Former unranked shrines in Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[Category:新上五島町の神社]] [[Category:Gion shrines]] [[Category:長崎県の旧無格社]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:八坂神社 (新上五島町友住郷)]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:orphaned pages as of April 8]] }} [[ja:八坂神社 (新上五島町友住郷)]] [[sn:Yasaka Shrine (Tomozumigo, Shinkamigoto Town)]] [[en:Yasaka Shrine (Tomozumigo, Shinkamigoto Town)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Yasaka Shrine (Tomozumigo, Shinkamigoto Town)}} {{translated page|ja|八坂神社 (新上五島町友住郷)|version=97032503|comment=Imported full ja history}}

{{Shinto Shrine | Name = Yasaka Shrine | image = [[File:IMG_tomosumi.JPG|250px]] | location = {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|長崎県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Shinkamigoto|ja|新上五島町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Tomozumigo, Tachiki 353 | coordinates = {{Coord|32|59|56.0|N|129|10|32.7|E|type:landmark_region:JP-42|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined Deities = {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞男神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Main deity)
{{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kanayamahiko|ja|金山彦神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Associated deities) | type = Former Unranked shrine | Founded = Unknown | festival = November 3 | Rituals = Arikawa Kagura (Annual Grand Festival) | Map = {{location map|Japan Nagasaki Prefecture|label=Yasaka Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }} '''Yasaka Shrine''' (八坂神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Shinkamigoto|ja|新上五島町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Nagasaki Prefecture, Japan. The shrine is situated near the summit of a small hill in Tomozumigo, with a lower shrine located in the residential area at the foot of the hill. == Enshrined Deities == The principal deity is {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞男神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The following deities are also enshrined: * {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (from {{ill|Kotohira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) * {{ill|Kanayamahiko|ja|金山彦神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (from Kotohira Shrine) * {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (from {{ill|Shirahime Shrine|ja|白峯神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (from {{ill|Ōyama Shrine|ja|大山神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) == History == The exact date of the shrine's founding and its origins are unknown. In May 1874 (Meiji 7), the shrine was officially recognized as an unranked shrine. On December 4, 1908 (Meiji 41), {{ill|Kotohira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} was merged into Yasaka Shrine. Later, Shirahime Shrine and Ōyama Shrine were also merged, though the exact dates are unclear. == Festivals == During the annual festival on November 2 and 3, the {{ill|Arikawa Kagura|ja|有川神楽|qq=draft|qq=draft}} is performed. This kagura is part of the nationally designated {{ill|Gojima Kagura|ja|五島神楽|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, recognized as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property. ;Major Festivals and Rituals * {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (February 18) * {{ill|Reitaisai|ja|例大祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (November 2–3) * {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (November 29) == Sub-Shrines == * {{ill|Kamakura Shrine|ja|鎌倉神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Kotohira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} File:IMG_tomosumi2.JPG|Kotohira Shrine File:IMG_tomosumi3.JPG|Kamakura Shrine (sub-shrine) == Other Shrines in Tomozumigo == Other shrines in Tomozumigo include: * {{ill|Ebisu Shrine|ja|恵比須神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Gyōja Mountain Shrine|ja|行者山社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Nearby shrines in adjacent areas: * {{ill|Harami Shrine|ja|孕神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (in Akao-go) * {{ill|Enohama Shrine|ja|江ノ濱神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (in Enohama-go) File:IMG_tomosumi4.JPG|Gyōja Mountain Shrine File:IMG_harami.JPG|Harami Shrine File:IMG_enohama.JPG|Enohama Shrine (Enohama-go) == References == * "Arikawa Town Local History" (平成6年), Arikawa Town Local History Compilation Committee * "Gojima Shrine Records" (昭和18th year , Reprint Edition), Jingikai Minamimatsuura County {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|八坂神社 (新上五島町友住郷)|version=97032503|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Yasaka Shrines]] [[Category:Shrines in Shinkamigoto]] [[Category:Gion Shrines]] [[Category:Former Unranked shrines in Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[Category:Gion shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:八坂神社 (新上五島町友住郷)]] [[Category:orphaned pages as of April 8]] }} [[ja:八坂神社 (新上五島町友住郷)]] [[sn:Yasaka Shrine (Tomozumigo, Shinkamigoto Town)]] [[en:Yasaka Shrine (Tomozumigo, Shinkamigoto Town)]]

{{Shinto Shrine | Name = Yasaka Shrine | image = [[File:IMG_tomosumi.JPG|250px]] | location = {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|長崎県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagasaki Prefecture|qq=}}, {{ill|Shinkamigoto|ja|新上五島町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinkamigoto|qq=}}, Tomozumigo, Tachiki 353 | coordinates = {{Coord|32|59|56.0|N|129|10|32.7|E|type:landmark_region:JP-42|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined Deities = {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞男神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}} (Main deity)
{{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|qq=}}, {{ill|Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime|lt=Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime|lt=Kanayamahiko|ja|金山彦神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanayamahiko|qq=}}, {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|qq=}}, {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|qq=}} (Associated deities) | type = Former Unranked shrine | Founded = Unknown | festival = November 3 | Rituals = Arikawa Kagura (Annual Grand Festival) | Map = {{location map|Japan Nagasaki Prefecture|label=Yasaka Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }} '''Yasaka Shrine''' (八坂神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} located in {{ill|Shinkamigoto|ja|新上五島町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinkamigoto|qq=}}, Nagasaki Prefecture, Japan. The shrine is situated near the summit of a small hill in Tomozumigo, with a lower shrine located in the residential area at the foot of the hill. == Enshrined Deities == The principal deity is {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞男神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}}. The following deities are also enshrined: * {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|qq=}} (from {{ill|Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira Shrine|qq=}}) * {{ill|Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime|lt=Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime|lt=Kanayamahiko|ja|金山彦神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanayamahiko|qq=}} (from Kotohira Shrine) * {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|qq=}} (from {{ill|Shirahime Shrine|ja|白峯神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirahime Shrine|qq=}}) * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|qq=}} (from {{ill|Ōyama Shrine|ja|大山神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyama Shrine|qq=}}) == History == The exact date of the shrine's founding and its origins are unknown. In May 1874 (Meiji 7), the shrine was officially recognized as an unranked shrine. On December 4, 1908 (Meiji 41), {{ill|Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira Shrine|qq=}} was merged into Yasaka Shrine. Later, Shirahime Shrine and Ōyama Shrine were also merged, though the exact dates are unclear. == Festivals == During the annual festival on November 2 and 3, the {{ill|Arikawa Kagura|ja|有川神楽|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arikawa Kagura|qq=}} is performed. This kagura is part of the nationally designated {{ill|Gojima Kagura|ja|五島神楽|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gojima Kagura|qq=}}, recognized as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property. ;Major Festivals and Rituals * {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinensai|qq=}} (February 18) * {{ill|Reitaisai|ja|例大祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reitaisai|qq=}} (November 2–3) * {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niinamesai|qq=}} (November 29) == Sub-Shrines == * {{ill|Kamakura Shrine|ja|鎌倉神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira {{Shinto Shrine | Name = Yasaka Shrine | image = [[File:IMG_tomosumi.JPG|250px]] | location = {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|長崎県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagasaki Prefecture|qq=}}, {{ill|Shinkamigoto|ja|新上五島町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinkamigoto|qq=}}, Tomozumigo, Tachiki 353 | coordinates = {{Coord|32|59|56.0|N|129|10|32.7|E|type:landmark_region:JP-42|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined Deities = {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞男神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}} (Main deity)
{{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|qq=}}, {{ill|Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime|lt=Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime|lt=Kanayamahiko|ja|金山彦神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanayamahiko|qq=}}, {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|qq=}}, {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|qq=}} (Associated deities) | type = Former Unranked shrine | Founded = Unknown | festival = November 3 | Rituals = Arikawa Kagura (Annual Grand Festival) | Map = {{location map|Japan Nagasaki Prefecture|label=Yasaka Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }} '''Yasaka Shrine''' (八坂神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} located in {{ill|Shinkamigoto|ja|新上五島町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinkamigoto|qq=}}, Nagasaki Prefecture, Japan. The shrine is situated near the summit of a small hill in Tomozumigo, with a lower shrine located in the residential area at the foot of the hill. == Enshrined Deities == The principal deity is {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞男神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}}. The following deities are also enshrined: * {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|qq=}} (from {{ill|Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira Shrine|qq=}}) * {{ill|Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime|lt=Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime|lt=Kanayamahiko|ja|金山彦神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanayamahiko|qq=}} (from Kotohira Shrine) * {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|qq=}} (from {{ill|Shirahime Shrine|ja|白峯神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirahime Shrine|qq=}}) * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|qq=}} (from {{ill|Ōyama Shrine|ja|大山神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyama Shrine|qq=}}) == History == The exact date of the shrine's founding and its origins are unknown. In May 1874 (Meiji 7), the shrine was officially recognized as an unranked shrine. On December 4, 1908 (Meiji 41), {{ill|Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira Shrine|qq=}} was merged into Yasaka Shrine. Later, Shirahime Shrine and Ōyama Shrine were also merged, though the exact dates are unclear. == Festivals == During the annual festival on November 2 and 3, the {{ill|Arikawa Kagura|ja|有川神楽|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arikawa Kagura|qq=}} is performed. This kagura is part of the nationally designated {{ill|Gojima Kagura|ja|五島神楽|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gojima Kagura|qq=}}, recognized as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property. ;Major Festivals and Rituals * {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinensai|qq=}} (February 18) * {{ill|Reisai|lt=Reisai|lt=Reitaisai|ja|例大祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reitaisai|qq=}} (November 2–3) * {{ill|Niiname-no-Matsuri|lt=Niiname-no-Matsuri|lt=Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niinamesai|qq=}} (November 29) == Sub-Shrines == * {{ill|Kamakura Shrine|ja|鎌倉神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira Shrine|qq=}} File:IMG_tomosumi2.JPG|Kotohira Shrine File:IMG_tomosumi3.JPG|Kamakura Shrine (sub-shrine) == Other Shrines in Tomozumigo == Other shrines in Tomozumigo include: * {{ill|Ebisu Shrine|ja|恵比須神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebisu Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Gyōja Mountain Shrine|ja|行者山社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gyōja Mountain Shrine|qq=}} Nearby shrines in adjacent areas: * {{ill|Harami Shrine|ja|孕神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harami Shrine|qq=}} (in Akao-go) * {{ill|Enohama Shrine|ja|江ノ濱神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enohama Shrine|qq=}} (in Enohama-go) File:IMG_tomosumi4.JPG|Gyōja Mountain Shrine File:IMG_harami.JPG|Harami Shrine File:IMG_enohama.JPG|Enohama Shrine (Enohama-go) == References == * "Arikawa Town Local History" (平成6年), Arikawa Town Local History Compilation Committee * "Gojima Shrine Records" (昭和18th year , Reprint Edition), Jingikai Minamimatsuura County {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|八坂神社 {{Shinto Shrine | Name = Yasaka Shrine | image = [[File:IMG_tomosumi.JPG|250px]] | location = [[:en:Nagasaki Prefecture|Nagasaki Prefecture]], [[:en:Shin-Kamigotō|Shinkamigoto]], Tomozumigo, Tachiki 353 | coordinates = {{Coord|32|59|56.0|N|129|10|32.7|E|type:landmark_region:JP-42|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined Deities = {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞男神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}} (Main deity)
{{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|qq=}}, {{ill|Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime|lt=Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime|lt=Kanayamahiko|ja|金山彦神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanayamahiko|qq=}}, {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|qq=}}, {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|qq=}} (Associated deities) | type = Former Unranked shrine | Founded = Unknown | festival = November 3 | Rituals = Arikawa Kagura (Annual Grand Festival) | Map = {{location map|Japan Nagasaki Prefecture|label=Yasaka Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }} '''Yasaka Shrine''' (八坂神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Shin-Kamigotō|Shinkamigoto]], Nagasaki Prefecture, Japan. The shrine is situated near the summit of a small hill in Tomozumigo, with a lower shrine located in the residential area at the foot of the hill. == Enshrined Deities == The principal deity is {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞男神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}}. The following deities are also enshrined: * {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|qq=}} (from {{ill|Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira Shrine|qq=}}) * {{ill|Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime|lt=Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime|lt=Kanayamahiko|ja|金山彦神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanayamahiko|qq=}} (from Kotohira Shrine) * {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|qq=}} (from {{ill|Shirahime Shrine|ja|白峯神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirahime Shrine|qq=}}) * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|qq=}} (from [[:en:Oyama Shrine|Ōyama Shrine]]) == History == The exact date of the shrine's founding and its origins are unknown. In May 1874 (Meiji 7), the shrine was officially recognized as an unranked shrine. On December 4, 1908 (Meiji 41), {{ill|Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira Shrine|qq=}} was merged into Yasaka Shrine. Later, Shirahime Shrine and Ōyama Shrine were also merged, though the exact dates are unclear. == Festivals == During the annual festival on November 2 and 3, the {{ill|Arikawa Kagura|ja|有川神楽|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arikawa Kagura|qq=}} is performed. This kagura is part of the nationally designated {{ill|Gojima Kagura|ja|五島神楽|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gojima Kagura|qq=}}, recognized as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property. ;Major Festivals and Rituals * [[:en:Kinen-sai|Kinensai]] (February 18) * {{ill|Reisai|lt=Reisai|lt=Reitaisai|ja|例大祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reitaisai|qq=}} (November 2–3) * [[:en:Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niinamesai]] (November 29) == Sub-Shrines == * {{ill|Kamakura Shrine|ja|鎌倉神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira Shrine|qq=}} File:IMG_tomosumi2.JPG|Kotohira Shrine File:IMG_tomosumi3.JPG|Kamakura Shrine (sub-shrine) == Other Shrines in Tomozumigo == Other shrines in Tomozumigo include: * {{ill|Ebisu Shrine|ja|恵比須神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebisu Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Gyōja Mountain Shrine|ja|行者山社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gyōja Mountain Shrine|qq=}} Nearby shrines in adjacent areas: * {{ill|Harami Shrine|ja|孕神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harami Shrine|qq=}} (in Akao-go) * {{ill|Enohama Shrine|ja|江ノ濱神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enohama Shrine|qq=}} (in Enohama-go) File:IMG_tomosumi4.JPG|Gyōja Mountain Shrine File:IMG_harami.JPG|Harami Shrine File:IMG_enohama.JPG|Enohama Shrine (Enohama-go) == References == * "Arikawa Town Local History" (平成6年), Arikawa Town Local History Compilation Committee * "Gojima Shrine Records" (昭和18th year , Reprint Edition), Jingikai Minamimatsuura County {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|八坂神社 (新上五島町友住郷)|version=97032503|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Yasaka Shrines]] [[Category:Shrines in Shinkamigoto]] [[Category:Gion Shrines]] [[Category:Former Unranked Shrines in Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[Category:Gion shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:八坂神社 (新上五島町友住郷)]] [[Category:orphaned pages as of April 8]] }} [[ja:八坂神社 (新上五島町友住郷)]] [[sn:Yasaka {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|qq=}}, {{ill|Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime|lt=Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime|lt=Kanayamahiko|ja|金山彦神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanayamahiko|qq=}}, {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|qq=}}, {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|qq=}} (Associated deities) | type = Former Unranked shrine | Founded = Unknown | festival = November 3 | Rituals = Arikawa Kagura (Annual Grand Festival) | Map = {{location map|Japan Nagasaki Prefecture|label=Yasaka Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }} '''Yasaka Shrine''' (八坂神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Shin-Kamigotō|Shinkamigoto]], Nagasaki Prefecture, Japan. The shrine is situated near the summit of a small hill in Tomozumigo, with a lower shrine located in the residential area at the foot of the hill. == Enshrined Deities == The principal deity is {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞男神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}}. The following deities are also enshrined: * {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|qq=}} (from {{ill|Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira Shrine|qq=}}) * {{ill|Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime|lt=Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime|lt=Kanayamahiko|ja|金山彦神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanayamahiko|qq=}} (from Kotohira Shrine) * {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|qq=}} (from {{ill|Shirahime Shrine|ja|白峯神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirahime Shrine|qq=}}) * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|qq=}} (from [[:en:Oyama Shrine|Ōyama Shrine]]) == History == The exact date of the shrine's founding and its origins are unknown. In May 1874 (Meiji 7), the shrine was officially recognized as an unranked shrine. On December 4, 1908 (Meiji 41), {{ill|Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira Shrine|qq=}} was merged into Yasaka Shrine. Later, Shirahime Shrine and Ōyama Shrine were also merged, though the exact dates are unclear. == Festivals == During the annual festival on November 2 and 3, the {{ill|Arikawa Kagura|ja|有川神楽|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arikawa Kagura|qq=}} is performed. This kagura is part of the nationally designated {{ill|Gojima Kagura|ja|五島神楽|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gojima Kagura|qq=}}, recognized as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property. ;Major Festivals and Rituals * [[:en:Kinen-sai|Kinensai]] (February 18) * {{ill|Reisai|lt=Reisai|lt=Reitaisai|ja|例大祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reitaisai|qq=}} (November
