[[commons:Category:Yasaka-jinja (Shinkamigoto)]][[ja:八坂神社 (新上五島町友住郷)]] [[commons:Category:Yasaka-jinja (Shinkamigoto)]][[ja:八坂神社 (新上五島町友住郷)]] [[sn:commons:Category:Yasaka-jinja (Shinkamigoto)]][[ja:八坂神社 (新上五島町友住郷)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11390704]] Wikidata name: Yasaka-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:29, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| | Name = Yasaka Shrine | image = File:IMG tomosumi.JPG | location = [[:en:Nagasaki Prefecture|Nagasaki Prefecture]], [[:en:Shin-Kamigotō|Shinkamigoto]], Tomozumigo, Tachiki 353 | coordinates = {{Coord|32.99888888888889|N|129.17575|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined Deities = {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞男神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} (Main deity)
{{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}}, {{ill|Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime|lt=Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime|lt=Kanayamahiko|ja|金山彦神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanayamahiko|qq=}}, {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主}}, {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}} (Associated deities) | type = Former Unranked shrine | Founded = Unknown | festival = November 3 | Rituals = Arikawa Kagura (Annual Grand Festival) | Map = {{location map|Japan Nagasaki Prefecture|label=Yasaka Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} | location = {{ill|Shinkamigoto|WD=Q1206790}} }} '''Yasaka Shrine''' (八坂神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Shin-Kamigotō|Shinkamigoto]], Nagasaki Prefecture, Japan. The shrine is situated near the summit of a small hill in Tomozumigo, with a lower shrine located in the residential area at the foot of the hill. == Enshrined Deities == The principal deity is {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞男神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}. The following deities are also enshrined: * {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}} (from {{ill|Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira Shrine|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}) * {{ill|Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime|lt=Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime|lt=Kanayamahiko|ja|金山彦神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanayamahiko|qq=}} (from Kotohira Shrine) * {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主}} (from {{ill|Shirahime Shrine|ja|白峯神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirahime Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}} (from [[:en:Oyama Shrine|Ōyama Shrine]]) == History == The exact date of the shrine's founding and its origins are unknown. In May 1874 (Meiji 7), the shrine was officially recognized as an unranked shrine. On December 4, 1908 (Meiji 41), {{ill|Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira Shrine|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} was merged into Yasaka Shrine. Later, Shirahime Shrine and Ōyama Shrine were also merged, though the exact dates are unclear. == Festivals == During the annual festival on November 2 and 3, the {{ill|Arikawa Kagura|ja|有川神楽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arikawa Kagura|1=Arikawa Kagura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11516390}} is performed. This kagura is part of the nationally designated {{ill|Gojima Kagura|ja|五島神楽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gojima Kagura|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, recognized as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property. ;Major Festivals and Rituals * [[:en:Kinen-sai|Kinensai]] (February 18) * {{ill|Reisai|lt=Reisai|lt=Reitaisai|ja|例大祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reitaisai|ja_comment=fixed the redirect|1=Reisai|2=ja|3=例祭|4=fr|5=Reisai|lt=Reitaisai}} (November 2–3) * [[:en:Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niinamesai]] (November 29) == Sub-Shrines == * {{ill|Kamakura Shrine|ja|鎌倉神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira Shrine|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} File:IMG_tomosumi2.JPG|Kotohira Shrine File:IMG_tomosumi3.JPG|Kamakura Shrine (sub-shrine) == Other Shrines in Tomozumigo == Other shrines in Tomozumigo include: * {{ill|Ebisu Shrine|ja|恵比須神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebisu Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to えびす神社}} * {{ill|Gyōja Mountain Shrine|ja|行者山社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gyōja Mountain Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} Nearby shrines in adjacent areas: * {{ill|Harami Shrine|ja|孕神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harami Shrine|1=Harami Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11447985}} (in Akao-go) * {{ill|Enohama Shrine|ja|江ノ濱神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enohama Shrine|1=Enohama Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11550594}} (in Enohama-go) File:IMG_tomosumi4.JPG|Gyōja Mountain Shrine File:IMG_harami.JPG|Harami Shrine File:IMG_enohama.JPG|Enohama Shrine (Enohama-go) == References == * "Arikawa Town Local History" (平成6年), Arikawa Town Local History Compilation Committee * "Gojima Shrine Records" (昭和18th year , Reprint Edition), Jingikai Minamimatsuura County {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|八坂神社 (新上五島町友住郷)|version=97032503|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shinkamigoto]] [[Category:Former Unranked Shrines in Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[ja:八坂神社 (新上五島町友住郷)]] [[Category:Yasaka-jinja]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shinkamigoto]] {{Commons category|Yasaka-jinja (Shinkamigoto)}} [[commons:Category:Yasaka-jinja (Shinkamigoto)]][[ja:八坂神社 (新上五島町友住郷)]] [[commons:Category:Yasaka-jinja (Shinkamigoto)]][[ja:八坂神社 (新上五島町友住郷)]] [[sn:commons:Category:Yasaka-jinja (Shinkamigoto)]][[ja:八坂神社 (新上五島町友住郷)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11390704]] Wikidata name: Yasaka-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:29, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| | Name = Yasaka Shrine | image = File:IMG tomosumi.JPG | location = [[:en:Nagasaki Prefecture|Nagasaki Prefecture]], [[:en:Shin-Kamigotō|Shinkamigoto]], Tomozumigo, Tachiki 353 | coordinates = {{Coord|32.99888888888889|N|129.17575|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined Deities = {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞男神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} (Main deity)
{{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}}, {{ill|Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime|lt=Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime|lt=Kanayamahiko|ja|金山彦神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanayamahiko|qq=}}, {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主}}, {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}} (Associated deities) | type = Former Unranked shrine | Founded = Unknown | festival = November 3 | Rituals = Arikawa Kagura (Annual Grand Festival) | Map = {{location map|Japan Nagasaki Prefecture|label=Yasaka Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} | location = {{ill|Shinkamigoto|WD=Q1206790}} }} '''Yasaka Shrine''' (八坂神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Shin-Kamigotō|Shinkamigoto]], Nagasaki Prefecture, Japan. The shrine is situated near the summit of a small hill in Tomozumigo, with a lower shrine located in the residential area at the foot of the hill. == Enshrined Deities == The principal deity is {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞男神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}. The following deities are also enshrined: * {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}} (from {{ill|Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira Shrine|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}) * {{ill|Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime|lt=Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime|lt=Kanayamahiko|ja|金山彦神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanayamahiko|qq=}} (from Kotohira Shrine) * {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主}} (from {{ill|Shirahime Shrine|ja|白峯神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirahime Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}} (from [[:en:Oyama Shrine|Ōyama Shrine]]) == History == The exact date of the shrine's founding and its origins are unknown. In May 1874 (Meiji 7), the shrine was officially recognized as an unranked shrine. On December 4, 1908 (Meiji 41), {{ill|Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira Shrine|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} was merged into Yasaka Shrine. Later, Shirahime Shrine and Ōyama Shrine were also merged, though the exact dates are unclear. == Festivals == During the annual festival on November 2 and 3, the {{ill|Arikawa Kagura|ja|有川神楽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arikawa Kagura|1=Arikawa Kagura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11516390}} is performed. This kagura is part of the nationally designated {{ill|Gojima Kagura|ja|五島神楽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gojima Kagura|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, recognized as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property. ;Major Festivals and Rituals * [[:en:Kinen-sai|Kinensai]] (February 18) * {{ill|Reisai|lt=Reisai|lt=Reitaisai|ja|例大祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reitaisai|ja_comment=fixed the redirect|1=Reisai|2=ja|3=例祭|4=fr|5=Reisai|lt=Reitaisai}} (November 2–3) * [[:en:Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niinamesai]] (November 29) == Sub-Shrines == * {{ill|Kamakura Shrine|ja|鎌倉神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira Shrine|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} File:IMG_tomosumi2.JPG|Kotohira Shrine File:IMG_tomosumi3.JPG|Kamakura Shrine (sub-shrine) == Other Shrines in Tomozumigo == Other shrines in Tomozumigo include: * {{ill|Ebisu Shrine|ja|恵比須神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebisu Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to えびす神社}} * {{ill|Gyōja Mountain Shrine|ja|行者山社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gyōja Mountain Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} Nearby shrines in adjacent areas: * {{ill|Harami Shrine|ja|孕神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harami Shrine|1=Harami Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11447985}} (in Akao-go) * {{ill|Enohama Shrine|ja|江ノ濱神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enohama Shrine|1=Enohama Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11550594}} (in Enohama-go) File:IMG_tomosumi4.JPG|Gyōja Mountain Shrine File:IMG_harami.JPG|Harami Shrine File:IMG_enohama.JPG|Enohama Shrine (Enohama-go) == References == [[co:commons:Category:Yasaka-jinja (Shinkamigoto)]][[ja:八坂神社 (新上五島町友住郷)]] [[co:commons:Category:Yasaka-jinja (Shinkamigoto)]][[ja:八坂神社 (新上五島町友住郷)]] [[sn:commons:Category:Yasaka-jinja (Shinkamigoto)]][[ja:八坂神社 (新上五島町友住郷)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11390704]] Wikidata name: Yasaka-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:29, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| | Name = Yasaka Shrine | image = File:IMG tomosumi.JPG | location = [[:en:Nagasaki Prefecture|Nagasaki Prefecture]], [[:en:Shin-Kamigotō|Shinkamigoto]], Tomozumigo, Tachiki 353 | coordinates = {{Coord|32.99888888888889|N|129.17575|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined Deities = {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞男神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} (Main deity)
{{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}}, {{ill|Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime|lt=Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime|lt=Kanayamahiko|ja|金山彦神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanayamahiko|qq=}}, {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主}}, {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}} (Associated deities) | type = Former Unranked shrine | Founded = Unknown | festival = November 3 | Rituals = Arikawa Kagura (Annual Grand Festival) | Map = {{location map|Japan Nagasaki Prefecture|label=Yasaka Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} | location = {{ill|Shinkamigoto|WD=Q1206790}} }} '''Yasaka Shrine''' (八坂神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Shin-Kamigotō|Shinkamigoto]], Nagasaki Prefecture, Japan. The shrine is situated near the summit of a small hill in Tomozumigo, with a lower shrine located in the residential area at the foot of the hill. == Enshrined Deities == The principal deity is {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞男神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}. The following deities are also enshrined: * {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}} (from {{ill|Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira Shrine|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}) * {{ill|Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime|lt=Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime|lt=Kanayamahiko|ja|金山彦神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanayamahiko|qq=}} (from Kotohira Shrine) * {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主}} (from {{ill|Shirahime Shrine|ja|白峯神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirahime Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}} (from [[:en:Oyama Shrine|Ōyama Shrine]]) == History == The exact date of the shrine's founding and its origins are unknown. In May 1874 (Meiji 7), the shrine was officially recognized as an unranked shrine. On December 4, 1908 (Meiji 41), {{ill|Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira Shrine|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} was merged into Yasaka Shrine. Later, Shirahime Shrine and Ōyama Shrine were also merged, though the exact dates are unclear. == Festivals == During the annual festival on November 2 and 3, the {{ill|Arikawa Kagura|ja|有川神楽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arikawa Kagura|1=Arikawa Kagura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11516390}} is performed. This kagura is part of the nationally designated {{ill|Gojima Kagura|ja|五島神楽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gojima Kagura|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, recognized as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property. ;Major Festivals and Rituals * [[:en:Kinen-sai|Kinensai]] (February 18) * {{ill|Reisai|lt=Reisai|lt=Reitaisai|ja|例大祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reitaisai|ja_comment=fixed the redirect|1=Reisai|2=ja|3=例祭|4=fr|5=Reisai|lt=Reitaisai}} (November 2–3) * [[:en:Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niinamesai]] (November 29) == Sub-Shrines == * {{ill|Kamakura Shrine|ja|鎌倉神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira Shrine|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} File:IMG_tomosumi2.JPG|Kotohira Shrine File:IMG_tomosumi3.JPG|Kamakura Shrine (sub-shrine) == Other Shrines in Tomozumigo == Other shrines in Tomozumigo include: * {{ill|Ebisu Shrine|ja|恵比須神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebisu Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to えびす神社}} * {{ill|Gyōja Mountain Shrine|ja|行者山社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gyōja Mountain Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} Nearby shrines in adjacent areas: * {{ill|Harami Shrine|ja|孕神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harami Shrine|1=Harami Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11447985}} (in Akao-go) * {{ill|Enohama Shrine|ja|江ノ濱神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enohama Shrine|1=Enohama Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11550594}} (in Enohama-go) File:IMG_tomosumi4.JPG|Gyōja Mountain Shrine File:IMG_harami.JPG|Harami Shrine File:IMG_enohama.JPG|Enohama Shrine (Enohama-go) == References == * "Arikawa Town Local History" (平成6年), Arikawa Town Local History Compilation Committee * "Gojima Shrine Records" (昭和18th year , Reprint Edition), Jingikai Minamimatsuura County {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|八坂神社 (新上五島町友住郷)|version=97032503|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:八坂神社 (新上五島町友住郷)]] {{Commons category|Yasaka-jinja (Shinkamigoto)}} {{wikidata link|Q11390704}} [[co:commons:Category:Yasaka-jinja (Shinkamigoto)]][[ja:八坂神社 (新上五島町友住郷)]] [[co:commons:Category:Yasaka-jinja (Shinkamigoto)]][[ja:八坂神社 (新上五島町友住郷)]] [[sn:commons:Category:Yasaka-jinja (Shinkamigoto)]][[ja:八坂神社 (新上五島町友住郷)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11390704]] Wikidata name: Yasaka-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:29, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| | Name = Yasaka Shrine | image = File:IMG tomosumi.JPG | location = {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|en|Nagasaki Prefecture|WD=Q169376|lt=Nagasaki Prefecture}}, {{ill|Shin-Kamigotō|en|Shin-Kamigotō|WD=Q1206790|lt=Shinkamigoto}}, Tomozumigo, Tachiki 353 | coordinates = {{Coord|32.99888888888889|N|129.17575|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined Deities = {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞男神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} (Main deity)
{{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}}, {{ill|Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime|lt=Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime|lt=Kanayamahiko|ja|金山彦神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanayamahiko|qq=}}, {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主}}, {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}} (Associated deities) | type = Former Unranked shrine | Founded = Unknown | festival = November 3 | Rituals = Arikawa Kagura (Annual Grand Festival) | Map = {{location map|Japan Nagasaki Prefecture|label=Yasaka Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} | location = {{ill|Shinkamigoto|WD=Q1206790}} }} '''Yasaka Shrine''' (八坂神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Shin-Kamigotō|en|Shin-Kamigotō|WD=Q1206790|lt=Shinkamigoto}}, Nagasaki Prefecture, Japan. The shrine is situated near the summit of a small hill in Tomozumigo, with a lower shrine located in the residential area at the foot of the hill. == Enshrined Deities == The principal deity is {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞男神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}. The following deities are also enshrined: * {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}} (from {{ill|Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira Shrine|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}) * {{ill|Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime|lt=Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime|lt=Kanayamahiko|ja|金山彦神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanayamahiko|qq=}} (from Kotohira Shrine) * {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主}} (from {{ill|Shirahime Shrine|ja|白峯神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirahime Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}} (from {{ill|Oyama Shrine|en|Oyama Shrine|WD=Q2042961|lt=Ōyama Shrine}}) == History == The exact date of the shrine's founding and its origins are unknown. In May 1874 (Meiji 7), the shrine was officially recognized as an unranked shrine. On December 4, 1908 (Meiji 41), {{ill|Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira Shrine|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} was merged into Yasaka Shrine. Later, Shirahime Shrine and Ōyama Shrine were also merged, though the exact dates are unclear. == Festivals == During the annual festival on November 2 and 3, the {{ill|Arikawa Kagura|ja|有川神楽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arikawa Kagura|1=Arikawa Kagura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11516390}} is performed. This kagura is part of the nationally designated {{ill|Gojima Kagura|ja|五島神楽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gojima Kagura|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, recognized as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property. ;Major Festivals and Rituals * {{ill|Kinen-sai|en|Kinen-sai|WD=Q118906710|lt=Kinensai}} (February 18) * {{ill|Reisai|lt=Reisai|lt=Reitaisai|ja|例大祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reitaisai|ja_comment=fixed the redirect|1=Reisai|2=ja|3=例祭|4=fr|5=Reisai|lt=Reitaisai}} (November 2–3) * {{ill|Niiname-no-Matsuri|en|Niiname-no-Matsuri|WD=Q11501518|lt=Niinamesai}} (November 29) == Sub-Shrines == * {{ill|Kamakura Shrine|ja|鎌倉神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira Shrine|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} File:IMG_tomosumi2.JPG|Kotohira Shrine File:IMG_tomosumi3.JPG|Kamakura Shrine (sub-shrine) == Other Shrines in Tomozumigo == Other shrines in Tomozumigo include: * {{ill|Ebisu Shrine|ja|恵比須神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebisu Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to えびす神社}} * {{ill|Gyōja Mountain Shrine|ja|行者山社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gyōja Mountain Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} Nearby shrines in adjacent areas: * {{ill|Harami Shrine|ja|孕神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harami Shrine|1=Harami Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11447985}} (in Akao-go) * {{ill|Enohama Shrine|ja|江ノ濱神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enohama Shrine|1=Enohama Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11550594}} (in Enohama-go) File:IMG_tomosumi4.JPG|Gyōja Mountain Shrine File:IMG_harami.JPG|Harami Shrine File:IMG_enohama.JPG|Enohama Shrine (Enohama-go) == References == * "Arikawa Town Local History" (平成6年), Arikawa Town Local History Compilation Committee * "Gojima Shrine Records" (昭和18th year , Reprint Edition), Jingikai Minamimatsuura County {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|八坂神社 (新上五島町友住郷)|version=97032503|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:八坂神社 (新上五島町友住郷)]] {{Commons category|Yasaka-jinja (Shinkamigoto)}} {{wikidata link|Q11390704}} [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Former Unranked Shrines in Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[Category:pages with conflicting qids]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shinkamigoto]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Yasaka-jinja]]

[[co:commons:Category:Yasaka-jinja (Shinkamigoto)]][[ja:八坂神社 (新上五島町友住郷)]] [[co:commons:Category:Yasaka-jinja (Shinkamigoto)]][[ja:八坂神社 (新上五島町友住郷)]] [[sn:commons:Category:Yasaka-jinja (Shinkamigoto)]][[ja:八坂神社 (新上五島町友住郷)]] {{Infobox religious building| | Name = Yasaka Shrine | image = File:IMG tomosumi.JPG | location = {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|en|Nagasaki Prefecture|WD=Q169376|lt=Nagasaki Prefecture}}, {{ill|Shin-Kamigotō|en|Shin-Kamigotō|WD=Q1206790|lt=Shinkamigoto}}, Tomozumigo, Tachiki 353 | coordinates = {{Coord|32.99888888888889|N|129.17575|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined Deities = {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞男神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} (Main deity)
{{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}}, {{ill|Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime|lt=Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime|lt=Kanayamahiko|ja|金山彦神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanayamahiko|qq=}}, {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主}}, {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}} (Associated deities) | type = Former Unranked shrine | Founded = Unknown | festival = November 3 | Rituals = Arikawa Kagura (Annual Grand Festival) | Map = {{location map|Japan Nagasaki Prefecture|label=Yasaka Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} | location = {{ill|Shinkamigoto|WD=Q1206790}} }} '''Yasaka Shrine''' (八坂神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Shin-Kamigotō|en|Shin-Kamigotō|WD=Q1206790|lt=Shinkamigoto}}, Nagasaki Prefecture, Japan. The shrine is situated near the summit of a small hill in Tomozumigo, with a lower shrine located in the residential area at the foot of the hill. == Enshrined Deities == The principal deity is {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞男神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}. The following deities are also enshrined: * {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}} (from {{ill|Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira Shrine|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}) * {{ill|Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime|lt=Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime|lt=Kanayamahiko|ja|金山彦神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanayamahiko|qq=}} (from Kotohira Shrine) * {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主}} (from {{ill|Shirahime Shrine|ja|白峯神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirahime Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}} (from {{ill|Oyama Shrine|en|Oyama Shrine|WD=Q2042961|lt=Ōyama Shrine}}) == History == The exact date of the shrine's founding and its origins are unknown. In May 1874 (Meiji 7), the shrine was officially recognized as an unranked shrine. On December 4, 1908 (Meiji 41), {{ill|Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira Shrine|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} was merged into Yasaka Shrine. Later, Shirahime Shrine and Ōyama Shrine were also merged, though the exact dates are unclear. == Festivals == During the annual festival on November 2 and 3, the {{ill|Arikawa Kagura|ja|有川神楽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arikawa Kagura|1=Arikawa Kagura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11516390}} is performed. This kagura is part of the nationally designated {{ill|Gojima Kagura|ja|五島神楽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gojima Kagura|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, recognized as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property. ;Major Festivals and Rituals * {{ill|Kinen-sai|en|Kinen-sai|WD=Q118906710|lt=Kinensai}} (February 18) * {{ill|Reisai|lt=Reisai|lt=Reitaisai|ja|例大祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reitaisai|ja_comment=fixed the redirect|1=Reisai|2=ja|3=例祭|4=fr|5=Reisai|lt=Reitaisai}} (November 2–3) * {{ill|Niiname-no-Matsuri|en|Niiname-no-Matsuri|WD=Q11501518|lt=Niinamesai}} (November 29) == Sub-Shrines == * {{ill|Kamakura Shrine|ja|鎌倉神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira Shrine|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} File:IMG_tomosumi2.JPG|Kotohira Shrine File:IMG_tomosumi3.JPG|Kamakura Shrine (sub-shrine) == Other Shrines in Tomozumigo == Other shrines in Tomozumigo include: * {{ill|Ebisu Shrine|ja|恵比須神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebisu Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to えびす神社}} * {{ill|Gyōja Mountain Shrine|ja|行者山社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gyōja Mountain Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} Nearby shrines in adjacent areas: * {{ill|Harami Shrine|ja|孕神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harami Shrine|1=Harami Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11447985}} (in Akao-go) * {{ill|Enohama Shrine|ja|江ノ濱神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enohama Shrine|1=Enohama Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11550594}} (in Enohama-go) File:IMG_tomosumi4.JPG|Gyōja Mountain Shrine File:IMG_harami.JPG|Harami Shrine File:IMG_enohama.JPG|Enohama Shrine (Enohama-go) == References == * "Arikawa Town Local History" (平成6年), Arikawa Town Local History Compilation Committee * "Gojima Shrine Records" (昭和18th year , Reprint Edition), Jingikai Minamimatsuura County {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|八坂神社 (新上五島町友住郷)|version=97032503|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:八坂神社 (新上五島町友住郷)]] {{Commons category|Yasaka-jinja (Shinkamigoto)}} {{wikidata link|Q11390704}} [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[co:commons:Category:Yasaka-jinja (Shinkamigoto)]][[ja:八坂神社 (新上五島町友住郷)]] [[co:commons:Category:Yasaka-jinja (Shinkamigoto)]][[ja:八坂神社 (新上五島町友住郷)]] [[Category:sn_crud|TESTcommons:Category:Yasaka-jinja (Shinkamigoto)]][[ja:八坂神社 (新上五島町友住郷)]] {{Infobox religious building| | Name = Yasaka Shrine | image = File:IMG tomosumi.JPG | location = {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|en|Nagasaki Prefecture|WD=Q169376|lt=Nagasaki Prefecture|1=Nagasaki Prefecture}}, {{ill|Shin-Kamigotō|en|Shin-Kamigotō|WD=Q1206790|lt=Shinkamigoto|1=Shin-Kamigotō}}, Tomozumigo, Tachiki 353 | coordinates = {{Coord|32.99888888888889|N|129.17575|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined Deities = {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞男神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} (Main deity)
{{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}}, {{ill|Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime|lt=Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime|lt=Kanayamahiko|ja|金山彦神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanayamahiko|qq=}}, {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}} (Associated deities) | type = Former Unranked shrine | Founded = Unknown | festival = November 3 | Rituals = Arikawa Kagura (Annual Grand Festival) | Map = {{location map|Japan Nagasaki Prefecture|label=Yasaka Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} | location = {{ill|Shinkamigoto|WD=Q1206790|1=Shin-Kamigotō}} }} '''Yasaka Shrine''' (八坂神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Shin-Kamigotō|en|Shin-Kamigotō|WD=Q1206790|lt=Shinkamigoto|1=Shin-Kamigotō}}, Nagasaki Prefecture, Japan. The shrine is situated near the summit of a small hill in Tomozumigo, with a lower shrine located in the residential area at the foot of the hill. == Enshrined Deities == The principal deity is {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞男神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}. The following deities are also enshrined: * {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}} (from {{ill|Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira Shrine|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}) * {{ill|Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime|lt=Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime|lt=Kanayamahiko|ja|金山彦神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanayamahiko|qq=}} (from Kotohira Shrine) * {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} (from {{ill|Shirahime Shrine|ja|白峯神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirahime Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}} (from {{ill|Oyama Shrine|en|Oyama Shrine|WD=Q2042961|lt=Ōyama Shrine|1=Oyama Shrine}}) == History == The exact date of the shrine's founding and its origins are unknown. In May 1874 (Meiji 7), the shrine was officially recognized as an unranked shrine. On December 4, 1908 (Meiji 41), {{ill|Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira Shrine|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} was merged into Yasaka Shrine. Later, Shirahime Shrine and Ōyama Shrine were also merged, though the exact dates are unclear. == Festivals == During the annual festival on November 2 and 3, the {{ill|Arikawa Kagura|ja|有川神楽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arikawa Kagura|1=Arikawa Kagura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11516390}} is performed. This kagura is part of the nationally designated {{ill|Gojima Kagura|ja|五島神楽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gojima Kagura|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, recognized as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property. ;Major Festivals and Rituals * {{ill|Kinen-sai|en|Kinen-sai|WD=Q118906710|lt=Kinensai|1=Kinensai}} (February 18) * {{ill|Reisai|lt=Reisai|lt=Reitaisai|ja|例大祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reitaisai|ja_comment=fixed the redirect|1=Reisai|2=ja|3=例祭|4=fr|5=Reisai|lt=Reitaisai}} (November 2–3) * {{ill|Niiname-no-Matsuri|en|Niiname-no-Matsuri|WD=Q11501518|lt=Niinamesai|1=Niiname-no-Matsuri}} (November 29) == Sub-Shrines == * {{ill|Kamakura Shrine|ja|鎌倉神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira Shrine|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} File:IMG_tomosumi2.JPG|Kotohira Shrine File:IMG_tomosumi3.JPG|Kamakura Shrine (sub-shrine) == Other Shrines in Tomozumigo == Other shrines in Tomozumigo include: * {{ill|Ebisu Shrine|ja|恵比須神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebisu Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to えびす神社}} * {{ill|Gyōja Mountain Shrine|ja|行者山社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gyōja Mountain Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} Nearby shrines in adjacent areas: * {{ill|Harami Shrine|ja|孕神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harami Shrine|1=Harami Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11447985}} (in Akao-go) * {{ill|Enohama Shrine|ja|江ノ濱神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enohama Shrine|1=Enohama Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11550594}} (in Enohama-go) File:IMG_tomosumi4.JPG|Gyōja Mountain Shrine File:IMG_harami.JPG|Harami Shrine File:IMG_enohama.JPG|Enohama Shrine (Enohama-go) == References == * "Arikawa Town Local History" (平成6年), Arikawa Town Local History Compilation Committee * "Gojima Shrine Records" (昭和18th year , Reprint Edition), Jingikai Minamimatsuura County {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|八坂神社 revision.text:
[[co:commons:Category:Yasaka-jinja (Shinkamigoto)]][[ja:八坂神社 (新上五島町友住郷)]] [[co:commons:Category:Yasaka-jinja (Shinkamigoto)]][[ja:八坂神社 (新上五島町友住郷)]] [[ja:八坂神社 (新上五島町友住郷)]] {{Infobox religious building| | Name = Yasaka Shrine | image = File:IMG tomosumi.JPG | location = {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|en|Nagasaki Prefecture|WD=Q169376|lt=Nagasaki Prefecture|1=Nagasaki Prefecture}}, {{ill|Shin-Kamigotō|en|Shin-Kamigotō|WD=Q1206790|lt=Shinkamigoto|1=Shin-Kamigotō}}, Tomozumigo, Tachiki 353 | coordinates = {{Coord|32.99888888888889|N|129.17575|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined Deities = {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞男神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} (Main deity)
{{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}}, {{ill|Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime|lt=Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime|lt=Kanayamahiko|ja|金山彦神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanayamahiko|qq=}}, {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}} (Associated deities) | type = Former Unranked shrine | Founded = Unknown | festival = November 3 | Rituals = Arikawa Kagura (Annual Grand Festival) | Map = {{location map|Japan Nagasaki Prefecture|label=Yasaka Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} | location = {{ill|Shinkamigoto|WD=Q1206790|1=Shin-Kamigotō}} }} '''Yasaka Shrine''' (八坂神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Shin-Kamigotō|en|Shin-Kamigotō|WD=Q1206790|lt=Shinkamigoto|1=Shin-Kamigotō}}, Nagasaki Prefecture, Japan. The shrine is situated near the summit of a small hill in Tomozumigo, with a lower shrine located in the residential area at the foot of the hill. == Enshrined Deities == The principal deity is {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞男神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}. The following deities are also enshrined: * {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}} (from {{ill|Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira Shrine|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}) * {{ill|Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime|lt=Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime|lt=Kanayamahiko|ja|金山彦神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanayamahiko|qq=}} (from Kotohira Shrine) * {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} (from {{ill|Shirahime Shrine|ja|白峯神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirahime Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}} (from {{ill|Oyama Shrine|en|Oyama Shrine|WD=Q2042961|lt=Ōyama Shrine|1=Oyama Shrine}}) == History == The exact date of the shrine's founding and its origins are unknown. In May 1874 (Meiji 7), the shrine was officially recognized as an unranked shrine. On December 4, 1908 (Meiji 41), {{ill|Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira Shrine|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} was merged into Yasaka Shrine. Later, Shirahime Shrine and Ōyama Shrine were also merged, though the exact dates are unclear. == Festivals == During the annual festival on November 2 and 3, the {{ill|Arikawa Kagura|ja|有川神楽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arikawa Kagura|1=Arikawa Kagura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11516390}} is performed. This kagura is part of the nationally designated {{ill|Gojima Kagura|ja|五島神楽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gojima Kagura|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, recognized as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property. ;Major Festivals and Rituals * {{ill|Kinen-sai|en|Kinen-sai|WD=Q118906710|lt=Kinensai|1=Kinensai}} (February 18) * {{ill|Reisai|lt=Reisai|lt=Reitaisai|ja|例大祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reitaisai|ja_comment=fixed the redirect|1=Reisai|2=ja|3=例祭|4=fr|5=Reisai|lt=Reitaisai}} (November 2–3) * {{ill|Niiname-no-Matsuri|en|Niiname-no-Matsuri|WD=Q11501518|lt=Niinamesai|1=Niiname-no-Matsuri}} (November 29) == Sub-Shrines == * {{ill|Kamakura Shrine|ja|鎌倉神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira Shrine|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} File:IMG_tomosumi2.JPG|Kotohira Shrine File:IMG_tomosumi3.JPG|Kamakura Shrine (sub-shrine) == Other Shrines in Tomozumigo == Other shrines in Tomozumigo include: * {{ill|Ebisu Shrine|ja|恵比須神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebisu Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to えびす神社}} * {{ill|Gyōja Mountain Shrine|ja|行者山社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gyōja Mountain Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} Nearby shrines in adjacent areas: * {{ill|Harami Shrine|ja|孕神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harami Shrine|1=Harami Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11447985}} (in Akao-go) * {{ill|Enohama Shrine|ja|江ノ濱神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enohama Shrine|1=Enohama Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11550594}} (in Enohama-go) File:IMG_tomosumi4.JPG|Gyōja Mountain Shrine File:IMG_harami.JPG|Harami Shrine File:IMG_enohama.JPG|Enohama Shrine (Enohama-go) == References == * "Arikawa Town Local History" (平成6年), Arikawa Town Local History Compilation Committee * "Gojima Shrine Records" (昭和18th year , Reprint Edition), Jingikai Minamimatsuura County {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|八坂神社 (新上五島町友住郷)|version=97032503|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:八坂神社 (新上五島町友住郷)]] {{Commons category|Yasaka-jinja (Shinkamigoto)}} {{wikidata link|Q11390704}} [[Category:Former Unranked Shrines in
